industry_index.industry_name: Durable Consumer Goods, Electronics & Lifestyle Products

workflows.industry_context: Energy services and trading firms run physical logistics, inventory, and field-service work across vessel and warehouse networks where contracts, schedules, and master data are maintained in spreadsheets bolted onto warehouse data. Field crews and ops teams capture and update records by hand, with little governance over who changed what or whether commitments are met.

use_cases.title: Vessel Schedule & Contract Writeback
use_cases.card_summary: Manage vessel schedules, contract terms, and cargo assignments in one governed workspace, writing inputs back to the warehouse alongside production data.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms in this sector often manage vessel schedules, contracts, and cargo assignments in spreadsheets layered on warehouse data, so ETB/ETA changes and contract terms drift out of sync with no record of who changed what.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine production shipping data with input tables for contracts, vessel masters, and schedules, letting ops maintain assignments and dates directly and write every change back with a history.

writeback_model.name: Schedule entries
writeback_model.description: where ops record each vessel assignment, cargo quantity, and ETB/ETA/BL date

writeback_model.name: Contract terms
writeback_model.description: the negotiated terms and counterparties maintained per shipment

use_cases.title: Field Tech Site Visit Capture

value_stat.lbl: data points verified per site visit

use_cases.card_summary: Give field techs a clean mobile form to verify and update standardized customer site data on-site, writing entries straight back to the catalog.
use_cases.pain_point: Field service teams in this sector often capture site data in spreadsheets or smartsheets, but a hands-on, non-spreadsheet-literate workforce abandons them, leaving customer records stale.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could present each customer's core (and customer-specific) data points in a simple mobile UI, let techs verify and update them on-site, and write entries directly back to the data catalog.

writeback_model.name: Site visit records
writeback_model.description: the verified and updated data points captured per customer per visit

writeback_model.name: Exception flags
writeback_model.description: fields that changed or need follow-up, with the tech and timestamp

use_cases.title: Multi-Warehouse Inventory Aging Control

value_stat.lbl: of stock at risk of expiry or write-off

use_cases.card_summary: Track inventory aging, expiration, and inbound/outbound flow across warehouses, capturing disposition decisions before stock is written off.
use_cases.pain_point: Operators frequently track inventory aging and expiry across multiple warehouses in spreadsheets, so soon-to-expire stock and slow movers surface too late to act on.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate inventory by receipt date across warehouses, flag aging and expiring lots, and let planners record disposition decisions that write back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Disposition decisions
writeback_model.description: the action taken on each aging or expiring lot and who decided

writeback_model.name: Transfer requests
writeback_model.description: proposed inter-warehouse moves to balance stock with approver

use_cases.title: Physical Commodity Position & Settlement
use_cases.card_summary: Reconcile traded volumes, prices, and counterparties against actual deliveries and capture settlement adjustments with sign-off.
use_cases.pain_point: Trading desks often reconcile contracted volumes and prices against actual deliveries in spreadsheets, so settlement disputes and pricing errors are caught late and resolved over email.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could match traded positions to delivered volumes, surface price and quantity variances, capture dispute disposition, and lock settled positions with an audit trail.

writeback_model.name: Settlement adjustments
writeback_model.description: approved corrections to volume or price per trade with the reason

writeback_model.name: Dispute notes
writeback_model.description: the disposition record for each contested settlement line

use_cases.title: Logistics Master Data Stewardship

value_stat.lbl: of address, vessel, and material masters

use_cases.card_summary: Maintain vessel, address, material, and conversion master data in a governed workflow instead of scattered reference spreadsheets.
use_cases.pain_point: Master data for vessels, addresses, materials, and conversions is often maintained in scattered reference spreadsheets, so the same entity is keyed differently across systems.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could present master records, let stewards propose adds and edits with validation, route approval, and write the governed values back to the warehouse for downstream use.

writeback_model.name: Master record changes
writeback_model.description: each proposed add or edit to a master entity with the requester

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each master change and when

use_cases.title: Inbound/Outbound Shipment Tracker

value_stat.lbl: of shipments tracked outside any system

use_cases.card_summary: Track inbound and outbound shipments across warehouses with status updates and exception notes captured by ops.
use_cases.pain_point: Shipment status across warehouses is often tracked on whiteboards and spreadsheets, so delays and missed receipts aren't visible until they cause a stockout.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track expected inbound and outbound shipments, let ops update status and log exceptions, and roll up a live network view of what is at risk.

writeback_model.name: Shipment status
writeback_model.description: the current state and updates for each inbound or outbound shipment

writeback_model.name: Exception notes
writeback_model.description: documented delays, shortages, or damage per shipment

use_cases.title: AP/AR Exception & Margin Review
use_cases.card_summary: Review AP/AR discrepancies and per-customer margin against operations data, capturing resolution notes in one workspace.
use_cases.pain_point: Finance teams in this sector often reconcile AP/AR and per-customer margin against operational data in spreadsheets, so discrepancies and thin-margin accounts go unexamined.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface AP/AR discrepancies and margin by customer alongside operational data, let analysts capture resolution notes, and write the disposition back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Discrepancy notes
writeback_model.description: the investigation findings and disposition for each AP/AR exception

writeback_model.name: Margin flags
writeback_model.description: accounts flagged for pricing or terms review with the reason

use_cases.title: Fuel & Materials Purchase Order Control
use_cases.card_summary: Route purchase orders for fuel and materials through approval against budget and supplier terms with a full trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Purchase orders for fuel and materials are often raised over email against a budget spreadsheet, so spend visibility and approval thresholds are inconsistent.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could intake PO requests, check them against budget and supplier terms, route approvals by tier, and feed approved orders into operations.

writeback_model.name: PO requests
writeback_model.description: each purchase order requested with supplier, amount, and justification

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each PO, at what tier, and the budget impact

use_cases.title: Customer Site Document Intake

value_stat.lbl: of site files missing a document

use_cases.card_summary: Upload site permits, contracts, and inspection records, extract key terms, and track each customer file to complete.
use_cases.pain_point: Site documents like permits, contracts, and inspection records are often filed in shared drives, so completeness per customer is unclear until an audit or renewal.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could ingest uploaded site documents, use AI to extract key terms, flag missing items against a checklist, and track follow-ups to a complete file.

writeback_model.name: Document checklist
writeback_model.description: which required documents are present, missing, or pending per customer site

writeback_model.name: Follow-up assignments
writeback_model.description: who owns chasing each missing item and the due date

value_case.leading_kpi: Site files complete at first pass

use_cases.title: Hedge & Exposure Position Annotation
use_cases.card_summary: Explain open commodity exposure and hedge positions with desk commentary beside the mark-to-market view.
use_cases.pain_point: Open exposure and hedge positions are often reported as a chart with the rationale living in a trader's head or a side email, so risk narratives get rebuilt each cycle.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit beside the exposure and hedge view, flag large movements, capture desk rationale, and assemble a reusable risk narrative for review.

writeback_model.name: Position commentary
writeback_model.description: the documented rationale behind each material exposure or hedge movement

workflows.industry_context: EnergyTech and grid software firms consolidate baseline energy, utility consumption, carbon, weather, and contract data to power monitoring and reporting, while their own GTM teams run partner, forecasting, and retention motions. Much of the manual overlay — savings overrides, partner targets, forecast assumptions, churn tags — lives in spreadsheets bolted onto the warehouse.

use_cases.title: Energy Savings Validation & Sign-Off
use_cases.card_summary: Consolidate baseline, consumption, weather, and carbon data to validate energy savings and capture sign-off for regulatory reporting.
use_cases.pain_point: Energy management teams often consolidate baseline, consumption, weather, and savings data across databases in spreadsheets, so the trail from raw data to reported savings is hard to defend.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate energy databases, normalize savings against baseline and weather, let analysts annotate anomalies, and capture sign-off to produce an audit-ready report.

writeback_model.name: Savings adjustments
writeback_model.description: documented overrides to calculated savings per contract with rationale

writeback_model.name: Sign-off record
writeback_model.description: who reviewed and approved each reporting period's savings figures

use_cases.title: Carbon & Emissions Reporting Workspace
use_cases.card_summary: Assemble carbon and emissions figures across facilities with documented assumptions and reviewer sign-off for disclosure.
use_cases.pain_point: Emissions reporting often stitches together facility data and emission factors in spreadsheets, leaving assumptions and approvals outside the numbers.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could pull consumption by facility, apply emission factors, capture assumption rationale, route reviewer sign-off, and export a disclosure-ready package.

writeback_model.name: Factor assumptions
writeback_model.description: the emission factor and method chosen per facility with rationale

writeback_model.name: Reviewer sign-offs
writeback_model.description: the record of who reviewed each disclosure figure and when

use_cases.title: Demand & Load Forecast Overrides
use_cases.card_summary: Let planners override granular demand and load forecasts and compare scenarios before they drive downstream capacity decisions.
use_cases.pain_point: Forecasting teams often pull model output into spreadsheets to apply manual overrides for capacity planning, with no scenario history or comparison.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface granular demand and load forecasts, let planners capture overrides in input tables, compare scenarios, and write the approved forecast back for downstream use.

writeback_model.name: Forecast overrides
writeback_model.description: manual adjustments to model load forecasts by segment or period

writeback_model.name: Scenario versions
writeback_model.description: saved forecast scenarios with their assumptions and approval state

use_cases.title: Partner Performance & Target Management
use_cases.card_summary: Consolidate partner metrics and let partner managers set targets, log commitments, and track performance against them.
use_cases.pain_point: Partner targets and review commitments are often kept in spreadsheets separate from the metrics, so follow-through between business reviews is invisible.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate partner GTM data, let managers set targets and log committed actions beside the metrics, and track them to the next review.

writeback_model.name: Partner targets
writeback_model.description: the agreed targets and quotas per partner for the period

writeback_model.name: Action commitments
writeback_model.description: what each partner manager committed to, owner, and due date

use_cases.title: Pipeline Probability Forecaster
use_cases.card_summary: Simulate pipeline movement using historical stage timing and probability, capturing inputs and writing scenario results back.
use_cases.pain_point: Sales forecasting often relies on flat funnel percentages, so teams resort to spreadsheets to model stage timing and probability with no place to store scenario versions.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let RevOps input probability and stage-timing assumptions, simulate pipeline movement, and write scenario results back for historical tracking and comparison.

writeback_model.name: Probability inputs
writeback_model.description: stage conversion and timing assumptions entered per scenario

writeback_model.name: Scenario results
writeback_model.description: the simulated forecast outputs saved for tracking

use_cases.title: Customer Churn Tagging & Save Queue

value_stat.lbl: annual logo churn in energy software

use_cases.card_summary: Tag accounts with churn probability, route at-risk customers to a save workflow, and track outcomes.
use_cases.pain_point: Retention teams often want to tag every customer with a churn probability but have nowhere governed to store the tags or manage the save motions that follow.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let CS tag accounts with churn probability, write the predictions back for monitoring, route at-risk accounts into a save queue, and track each to outcome.

writeback_model.name: Churn tags
writeback_model.description: the assigned churn probability and reason per account

writeback_model.name: Save plans
writeback_model.description: the planned actions and outcome for each at-risk account

use_cases.title: Utility Cost Allocation & Showback
use_cases.card_summary: Allocate utility and operating costs to buildings, contracts, or customers with owner annotations and a trusted showback view.
use_cases.pain_point: Allocating utility and operating cost to buildings, contracts, or customers is a recurring spreadsheet exercise that no one fully trusts.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could ingest utility billing, apply allocation rules, let owners review and annotate unallocated cost, and publish a trusted showback view.

writeback_model.name: Allocation overrides
writeback_model.description: manual mapping of shared or unallocated cost to the right owner

writeback_model.name: Owner annotations
writeback_model.description: explanations attached to anomalous cost lines

use_cases.title: Data Quality Validation Queue

value_stat.lbl: ingest of meter and sensor data

use_cases.card_summary: Surface ingest and validation issues across energy data feeds and let data teams triage, annotate, and resolve them.
use_cases.pain_point: Data teams ingesting meter, weather, and consumption feeds often track validation issues in tickets and spreadsheets, so resolution status and recurring problems are unclear.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface validation failures across feeds, let data teams triage and annotate them, and write resolution status back to track recurring issues.

writeback_model.name: Issue disposition
writeback_model.description: the triage decision and resolution note per validation failure

writeback_model.name: Owner assignments
writeback_model.description: who is working each issue and the status

use_cases.title: Contract & Renewal Term Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Digitize energy service contract terms, escalators, and renewal dates, flagging upcoming actions before they lapse.
use_cases.pain_point: Service contract terms, escalators, and renewal dates are buried in documents, so renewals and price escalations are managed reactively in spreadsheets.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could digitize contract terms, track renewal and escalator dates, flag upcoming actions, and capture the outcome of each renewal motion.

writeback_model.name: Contract terms
writeback_model.description: the key terms, escalators, and renewal dates per contract

writeback_model.name: Renewal outcomes
writeback_model.description: the action taken and result for each upcoming renewal

use_cases.title: Building Portfolio Performance Annotation

value_stat.lbl: performance gap top vs bottom site

use_cases.card_summary: Pair building energy KPIs with facility-manager commentary and committed actions, tracked to outcome across the portfolio.
use_cases.pain_point: Portfolio energy reviews produce dashboards with no structured place to capture what facility managers commit to do, so follow-through is invisible month to month.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit alongside building energy KPIs, let facility managers log commentary and committed actions, and track them to the next review.

writeback_model.name: Action commitments
writeback_model.description: what each facility manager committed to, owner, and due date

writeback_model.name: Performance commentary
writeback_model.description: root-cause notes attached to each period's building performance

workflows.industry_context: Oil and gas operators run production, drilling, HSE, and field-calibration work across regions where field crews capture readings and the back office reconciles to budget and finance systems. A large layer of operational and corporate process — well data entry, meter calibration, department mapping, access approvals, scenario planning — lives in spreadsheets and email-driven workflows with weak audit trails.

use_cases.title: Well Performance Data Capture

value_stat.lbl: real-time drilling metrics entered in field

use_cases.card_summary: Let field operators input drilling metrics like ROP and MSE and monitor efficiency across rigs and wells in one workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Drilling teams often capture well metrics in spreadsheets per rig, so performance indicators like ROP and MSE aren't comparable across wells or fed back into optimization.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let field operators input well, section, and rig data, track ROP, MSE, and cycle times across rigs, and write captured metrics back for performance optimization.

writeback_model.name: Well data entries
writeback_model.description: the drilling metrics, sections, and rig assignments captured per well

writeback_model.name: Performance flags
writeback_model.description: wells or rigs flagged as underperforming with the reason

use_cases.title: Meter Calibration Scheduling & Capture

value_stat.lbl: of field meters on calibration cycles

use_cases.card_summary: Track meter calibration schedules, let field techs enter calibration data, and write results back to production systems.
use_cases.pain_point: Meter calibration schedules and field results are often tracked on paper and spreadsheets, so overdue calibrations and measurement errors surface late.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could manage calibration schedules, let field techs input calibration data and review reports, flag out-of-tolerance meters, and write results back to production systems.

writeback_model.name: Calibration records
writeback_model.description: the readings, tolerance result, and tech for each meter calibration

writeback_model.name: Schedule status
writeback_model.description: which meters are due, completed, or overdue per period

use_cases.title: Production Budget vs Actual Review

value_stat.lbl: budget vs actual close by region

use_cases.card_summary: Compare production and fuel output against budget by region, capture variance explanations, and feed forecasting.
use_cases.pain_point: Production and fuel-output budget-versus-actual is often reconciled in spreadsheets by region, so the reasons behind variances and completion misses aren't captured.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could compare production and fuel output to budget by region, surface variances, and capture root-cause annotations that improve the next forecast.

writeback_model.name: Variance explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of each material production or budget variance

writeback_model.name: Forecast adjustments
writeback_model.description: revised production assumptions written back per region

use_cases.title: HSE Incident & Compliance Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Log HSE incidents and compliance checks by site, document corrective actions, and track them to closure.
use_cases.pain_point: HSE incidents and compliance checks are often recorded across forms and spreadsheets, making it hard to prove every corrective action was completed for regulators.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could capture HSE incidents and compliance checks by site, classify severity, assign and track corrective actions, and produce a regulator-ready compliance view.

writeback_model.name: Incident records
writeback_model.description: the details, severity, and site for each HSE event

writeback_model.name: Corrective actions
writeback_model.description: the assigned remediation, owner, and closure status

use_cases.title: HR-to-Finance Department Mapping
use_cases.card_summary: Keep HR department hierarchies aligned with finance cost centers, flag mismatches, and manage approvals for changes.
use_cases.pain_point: Keeping HR department hierarchies aligned with finance cost centers is often a manual spreadsheet reconciliation, so naming mismatches and pending approvals pile up.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track HR and finance department structures side by side, flag mismatches, capture mapping change requests, and route approvals to keep them aligned.

writeback_model.name: Mapping changes
writeback_model.description: each proposed alignment between an HR department and a finance cost center

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each department create, deactivate, or remap and when

use_cases.title: Access Request & Approval Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Let employees submit access requests, route them to approvers, and track status with admin oversight.
use_cases.pain_point: Access requests are often submitted over email with approvals scattered, so it's hard to show who approved which access and when.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could intake access requests through a form, route them through an approval pipeline, track status, and give admins oversight of all requests with a clean audit trail.

writeback_model.name: Access requests
writeback_model.description: each submitted request with the employee, system, and justification

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: the approve, reject, or pending decision per request with approver

use_cases.title: Growth Scenario Planning Workspace
use_cases.card_summary: Let segment leaders input growth scenarios, adjust win rates and metrics, and write versioned scenarios back to the warehouse.
use_cases.pain_point: Growth planning across segments is often done by manipulating exported data in Excel, making version control and collaboration painful with no stored scenario history.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let segment leaders input growth scenarios and adjust metrics in input tables, store versioned scenarios in the warehouse, and compare them in one evergreen workspace.

writeback_model.name: Scenario inputs
writeback_model.description: the growth assumptions, win rates, and metrics entered per segment

writeback_model.name: Scenario versions
writeback_model.description: saved planning versions with their assumptions and timestamp

use_cases.title: EPM Responsibility Assignment
use_cases.card_summary: Map employees to cost centers and org structures for performance management, tracking changes and approvals with backups.
use_cases.pain_point: Mapping employees to cost centers and organizational hierarchies for performance management is often a spreadsheet exercise prone to mismatches and lost versions.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate HR and finance structures, let finance assign responsibilities and hierarchies, flag mismatches, route approvals, and retain versioned backups.

writeback_model.name: Responsibility assignments
writeback_model.description: the employee-to-cost-center and hierarchy mappings maintained per period

writeback_model.name: Change approvals
writeback_model.description: the record of approved structure changes and backups

use_cases.title: Field Service Work Order & Downtime Log

value_stat.lbl: of output lost to unplanned downtime

use_cases.card_summary: Log field work orders and downtime events against assets to tie maintenance to lost production.
use_cases.pain_point: Field maintenance and downtime are often recorded on paper or makeshift apps, so it's hard to connect work orders to the production they cost.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let field teams log downtime and work orders on mobile, capture root cause, and tie each event to estimated lost production.

writeback_model.name: Work orders
writeback_model.description: each maintenance task, asset, status, and assignee

writeback_model.name: Downtime events
writeback_model.description: the cause, duration, and estimated lost output per event

use_cases.title: Service Account Membership Governance
use_cases.card_summary: Review and write back user and service account memberships against a governance model in one workspace.
use_cases.pain_point: User and service account memberships are often managed in scattered grouping tables and spreadsheets, so access governance reviews are hard to evidence.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could display user and service account memberships against a governance model, let admins review and adjust them, and write membership changes back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Membership changes
writeback_model.description: each user or service account membership added, removed, or recertified

writeback_model.name: Review record
writeback_model.description: who recertified each membership and when

workflows.industry_context: Renewable developers and operators juggle project finance, generation forecasting, tariff and credit management, and field operations across data that spans CRM, asset platforms, and lender systems. Many teams stretch BI or low-code tools into makeshift data-entry apps.

use_cases.title: Debt Portfolio & Covenant Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Consolidate loan agreements and debt instruments to track capital raises, draws, and covenant compliance.
use_cases.pain_point: Developers often track debt facilities across CRM, lender platforms, and spreadsheets, making covenant compliance and capital-raise status hard to see in one place.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate loan agreement data with debt instrument records, give visibility into the debt portfolio and capital raises, and flag covenant thresholds with documented status.

writeback_model.name: Covenant status notes
writeback_model.description: the documented compliance position for each facility per period

writeback_model.name: Capital raise tracking
writeback_model.description: the stage and amount of each in-progress financing

use_cases.title: Smart Export Tariff Management

value_stat.lbl: tariff rates change by scheme and period

use_cases.card_summary: Manage smart export tariff data and calculations so the operations team can input and track tariff figures in one place.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams often lack an efficient system for export tariff data, relying on manual processes and disparate tools to track figures and calculations.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could provide an interactive workflow to manage smart export tariff data and calculations, letting operations input and track tariff information directly.

writeback_model.name: Tariff figures
writeback_model.description: the rate inputs and parameters for each export tariff scheme

writeback_model.name: Calculation overrides
writeback_model.description: manual adjustments to export value calculations with rationale

use_cases.title: Generation Forecast vs Actual Review
use_cases.card_summary: Compare forecast output to actual generation by asset, capture explanations for variances, and feed forecasting.
use_cases.pain_point: Output forecasts and actuals are often compared in spreadsheets, so the reasons behind underperformance aren't captured or fed back into the model.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could compare forecast and actual generation by asset, surface variances, and capture root-cause annotations that improve the next forecast.

writeback_model.name: Variance explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of each material generation shortfall or surplus

use_cases.title: REC Generation & Retirement Ledger
use_cases.card_summary: Track renewable energy credit generation, sales, and retirements against generation in an auditable ledger.
use_cases.pain_point: REC issuance, sales, and retirements are frequently tracked in spreadsheets disconnected from generation data, risking double-counting and missed revenue.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could derive expected RECs from generation, record issuance and sales, reconcile against the registry, and report the current position.

writeback_model.name: REC transactions
writeback_model.description: each issuance, sale, or retirement with counterparty and date

writeback_model.name: Registry reconciliation
writeback_model.description: matched vs unmatched positions against the external registry

use_cases.title: Interconnection Queue Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Track each project's interconnection milestones, studies, and deposits across a long, multi-party process.
use_cases.pain_point: Interconnection progress spans utilities, ISOs, and consultants over years, and is usually tracked in spreadsheets where milestones and deposits slip unnoticed.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track queue position, study milestones, and deposits per project, flag slipping dates, and roll up portfolio interconnection status.

writeback_model.name: Milestone updates
writeback_model.description: the status and date of each interconnection study or deliverable

writeback_model.name: Deposit tracking
writeback_model.description: amounts posted and at risk per project

use_cases.title: O&M Work Order & Downtime Log
use_cases.card_summary: Log field work orders and downtime events against assets to tie maintenance to lost generation.
use_cases.pain_point: Field maintenance and downtime are often recorded on paper or makeshift apps, so it's hard to connect work orders to the generation they cost.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let field teams log downtime and work orders on mobile, capture root cause, and tie each event to estimated lost generation.

writeback_model.name: Work orders
writeback_model.description: each maintenance task, asset, status, and assignee

writeback_model.name: Downtime events
writeback_model.description: the cause, duration, and estimated lost output per event

use_cases.title: PPA & Contract Obligation Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Track power purchase agreement terms, delivery obligations, and pricing escalators against actual delivery.
use_cases.pain_point: Long-dated PPA terms, escalators, and delivery obligations are buried in contracts, so compliance and revenue tracking happen reactively in spreadsheets.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could digitize PPA terms, track delivery against obligations, apply pricing escalators, and flag shortfall or penalty risk.

writeback_model.name: Contract terms
writeback_model.description: the key obligations, escalators, and penalties per PPA

writeback_model.name: Delivery tracking
writeback_model.description: actual delivered volume vs contractual obligation per period

use_cases.title: Capital Project Budget Control
use_cases.card_summary: Track project budgets, commitments, and change orders against plan with approval for variances.
use_cases.pain_point: Project budgets and change orders are typically managed in spreadsheets, so cost overruns surface late and approvals lack a trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track budget, commitments, and change orders per project, route variance approvals, and report actuals against plan.

writeback_model.name: Change orders
writeback_model.description: each budget change requested, with cause and amount

writeback_model.name: Variance approvals
writeback_model.description: who approved each over-plan amount and when

use_cases.title: Curtailment Event Tracking

value_stat.lbl: of output curtailed in constrained grids

use_cases.card_summary: Log grid curtailment events, quantify lost generation, and document compensation claims.
use_cases.pain_point: Curtailment events are often noted ad hoc, so lost generation and any compensation owed go under-quantified.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could record curtailment events, quantify lost generation, document the grid instruction, and assemble compensation claims.

writeback_model.name: Curtailment events
writeback_model.description: the cause, duration, and lost MWh of each event with the grid instruction

writeback_model.name: Compensation claims
writeback_model.description: amounts claimed and status per event

use_cases.title: Land Lease & Royalty Administration
use_cases.card_summary: Administer land leases, escalators, and royalty payments with reminders and an approval trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Land leases with escalators and royalty terms are tracked in spreadsheets, so payment dates, escalations, and renewals slip without warning.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could administer lease and royalty terms, calculate payments due, flag escalations and renewals, and capture payment approval.

writeback_model.name: Lease terms
writeback_model.description: the payment, escalator, and renewal terms per parcel

writeback_model.name: Payment approvals
writeback_model.description: approved disbursements per period with approver

workflows.industry_context: Regulated electric, gas, and water utilities run outage, field, vegetation, and meter-to-cash operations across a patchwork of OMS, GIS, CIS, and asset systems that rarely talk to each other, so coordination falls back to email, phone trees, and spreadsheets. Program teams extend core contract and accounting data with custom spreadsheet layers, and regulators scrutinize program effectiveness in rate cases, making governed, auditable workflows valuable.

use_cases.title: Outage Coordination & Restoration Log

use_cases.title: Pricing Experiment Decision Log
use_cases.card_summary: Capture experiment hypotheses, treatment results, and ship/kill decisions for pricing and offer tests.
use_cases.pain_point: A/B test results for pricing and offers are often reviewed in dashboards while the decision rationale lives in someone's head, so prior experiment learnings get lost.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit beside experiment analytics, let teams log the ship/kill decision and rationale for each test, and maintain a searchable decision history.

writeback_model.name: Experiment decisions
writeback_model.description: the documented ship/kill call and rationale for each test

writeback_model.name: Follow-up experiments
writeback_model.description: the next tests queued from each decision

workflows.industry_context: Fintech and payments companies blend live system-of-record data with human inputs for planning, SLA management, and engineering delivery, but a lot of target-setting, partner-SLA tracking, and forecast roll-ups still happen in spreadsheets and side tools. The recurring-revenue and high-velocity engineering model makes small process gaps compound across trading, technology, and brokerage operations.

use_cases.title: Bottom-Up Sales Forecast Roll-Up
use_cases.card_summary: Let reps input commit/likely/best-case forecasts alongside CRM data and roll them up through the management hierarchy.
use_cases.pain_point: Sales teams in this sector often manage bottom-up forecasts across disparate systems, manually merging rep inputs with CRM data and losing the roll-up trail through management.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could put an input element beside governed CRM data, let reps submit commit/most-likely/best-case forecasts, and roll them up cleanly through the management hierarchy.

writeback_model.name: Forecast inputs
writeback_model.description: each rep's commit, likely, and best-case numbers per opportunity or segment

writeback_model.name: Manager adjustments
writeback_model.description: the documented overrides and approvals applied at each level of the roll-up

use_cases.title: Cash Flow Forecast & Positioning
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze current cash positions and build forward-looking projections with finance-owned assumptions and overrides.
use_cases.pain_point: Cash positioning and forecasting in payments businesses often blends warehouse data with manual assumptions in spreadsheets, with no shared place to capture overrides or compare scenarios.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine cash-flow data with finance-entered assumptions, let treasury capture overrides in input tables, compare scenarios, and publish a trusted forward projection.

writeback_model.name: Forecast assumptions
writeback_model.description: the driver assumptions and overrides applied to each cash projection

writeback_model.name: Scenario versions
writeback_model.description: the saved cash scenarios with their inputs and outcomes

use_cases.title: Partner SLA Violation Workspace
use_cases.card_summary: Track partner API availability and Tier 1/2 SLA violations, annotate incidents, and manage remediation owners.
use_cases.pain_point: Partner SLA performance and incident follow-ups are often spread across monitoring tools, spreadsheets, and email, so violations and credits aren't tracked to closure.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface API availability and SLA violations, let teams document incident root cause, assign remediation owners, and track each violation to closure with an audit trail.

writeback_model.name: Incident annotations
writeback_model.description: the documented root cause and impact for each SLA violation

writeback_model.name: Remediation items
writeback_model.description: the owner, action, and status of each follow-up

use_cases.title: Churn Prediction Input Workbench
use_cases.card_summary: Let teams enter new account data into a logistic model and instantly see predicted churn and variable impacts.
use_cases.pain_point: Product teams often need to score new account scenarios against a churn model, but today that means repeated Excel exports rather than an interactive interface.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could expose a logistic churn model interface, let users enter new account data, instantly return predicted churn likelihood and variable impacts, and save scenarios for comparison.

writeback_model.name: Scenario inputs
writeback_model.description: the new account attributes users enter to score against the model

writeback_model.name: Prediction history
writeback_model.description: the saved predictions and variable impacts per scenario

use_cases.title: Engineering Delivery Commitment Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Capture PI baseline commitments and track epic achievement, bug trends, and velocity against actuals.
use_cases.pain_point: Delivery commitments and baselines are often tracked in spreadsheets bolted onto Jira, so baseline-versus-actual achievement and bug trends are hard to reconcile each increment.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could pull Jira delivery metrics, let teams input baseline commitments, track actuals against them with bug-trend and velocity views, and roll up achievement by squad.

writeback_model.name: Baseline commitments
writeback_model.description: the committed scope and dates entered per squad and increment

writeback_model.name: Achievement notes
writeback_model.description: documented explanations for variances between baseline and actual

use_cases.title: Executive KPI Target Input Suite
use_cases.card_summary: Let leaders input planning targets and board commitments alongside live KPIs across trading, tech, and operations.
use_cases.pain_point: Cross-functional KPI planning data, like product parity targets and board project commitments, is often gathered in spreadsheets disconnected from the live KPIs it's measured against.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface KPIs across trading, technology, and brokerage operations, let leaders input targets, baselines, and board project linkages, and blend them with live warehouse data.

writeback_model.name: Planning targets
writeback_model.description: the entered targets and baselines per domain and period

writeback_model.name: Board commitments
writeback_model.description: the linkage between board projects and the KPIs that track them

use_cases.title: Venue Concentration Review
use_cases.card_summary: Monitor execution venue concentration, annotate threshold breaches, and document mitigation decisions.
use_cases.pain_point: Venue concentration is often reviewed in static reports, with the explanation and mitigation decisions for any concentration breach living outside the numbers.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface execution venue concentration, flag threshold breaches, capture context and mitigation decisions, and track them through to the next review.

writeback_model.name: Concentration annotations
writeback_model.description: the documented context behind each venue concentration breach

writeback_model.name: Mitigation decisions
writeback_model.description: the chosen action and owner for each flagged concentration

use_cases.title: Self-Clearing Settlement Monitor
use_cases.card_summary: Monitor open settlements and self-clearing rates, work exceptions, and document disposition before cutoff.
use_cases.pain_point: Open settlements and self-clearing exceptions are often worked across the clearing system and spreadsheets, so aging and ownership are unclear under compressed settlement windows.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface open settlements and self-clearing rates, let operations document investigation and disposition on exceptions, and track clearance against cutoff.

writeback_model.name: Settlement notes
writeback_model.description: the investigation and disposition for each open settlement exception

writeback_model.name: Clearance status
writeback_model.description: the current state and age of each settlement item

use_cases.title: Model Variable Testing Platform
use_cases.card_summary: Let technical teams test model variables interactively, log configurations, and compare outcomes without re-exporting.
use_cases.pain_point: Technical teams often test model variables by exporting data and rebuilding in notebooks, with no shared record of which variable configurations were tried and how they performed.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let teams adjust model variable configurations against live data, compare outcomes, and log the chosen configuration so testing is shared and reproducible.

writeback_model.name: Variable configurations
writeback_model.description: the tested combinations of model inputs and parameters

writeback_model.name: Outcome notes
writeback_model.description: the recorded performance and decision for each configuration

use_cases.title: Product Parity Target Submission
use_cases.card_summary: Submit and adjust product-parity analysis targets, route review, and track actual parity against plan.
use_cases.pain_point: Product parity targets are often set in spreadsheets and emailed for sign-off, with no structured submission, approval, or trail tying targets to actual parity.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let product teams submit parity analysis targets, route them for review and approval, and track actual parity against the approved plan in one workspace.

writeback_model.name: Parity targets
writeback_model.description: the submitted parity targets per product area and period

writeback_model.name: Target approvals
writeback_model.description: the record of which target set was reviewed and approved

workflows.industry_context: InsurTech platforms run digital lead-to-bind funnels, carrier-appetite analytics, and policy/premium operations across carrier, broker, and processor data. Much of the event modeling, manual bind tracking, carrier-threshold setting, and client forecast exchange still happens in spreadsheets and email round-trips, where there is no shared, governed place to capture inputs and impact.

use_cases.title: Lead-to-Bind Funnel Event Modeling
use_cases.card_summary: Track the lead-quote-bind-decline funnel and model the bind and premium impact of carrier launches and turnoffs.
use_cases.pain_point: InsurTech teams often monitor the LQBD funnel in dashboards while logging carrier events and modeling their impact in side spreadsheets, so the link between an event and the bind/premium change is lost.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface the LQBD funnel by carrier, state, and period, let teams log product events and manual bind averages, and model the estimated bind and premium impact with write-back.

writeback_model.name: Carrier events
writeback_model.description: the logged carrier launches, turnoffs, and their effective dates

writeback_model.name: Impact assumptions
writeback_model.description: the manual bind averages and modeled impact entered per event

use_cases.title: Carrier Appetite & Threshold Manager
use_cases.card_summary: Set carrier and wholesaler writing thresholds against book-of-business data and monitor concentration growth.
use_cases.pain_point: Carrier appetite and concentration limits are often tracked in spreadsheets disconnected from invoice and book-of-business data, so writing thresholds and growth signals are hard to manage in one place.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine carrier appetite with book-of-business and invoice data, let underwriters set writing thresholds, flag concentration breaches, and track growth against appetite.

writeback_model.name: Writing thresholds
writeback_model.description: the carrier and wholesaler concentration limits set by underwriters

writeback_model.name: Appetite notes
writeback_model.description: documented changes in carrier appetite and the rationale

use_cases.title: Client Forecast Review Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Let clients input forecast adjustments directly instead of exchanging spreadsheets, writing back live to the warehouse.
use_cases.pain_point: Client forecast review often happens through spreadsheet exchanges, a slow round-trip with no real-time dialogue and delays of several days before data is updated.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let clients enter forecast adjustments directly through a governed interface, capture rationale, and update warehouse data live, replacing the spreadsheet exchange with a real-time workflow.

writeback_model.name: Client forecast adjustments
writeback_model.description: the changes clients submit directly against the live forecast

writeback_model.name: Review decisions
writeback_model.description: the internal record of accepted or revised client inputs

use_cases.title: Policy Premium & Commission Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Consolidate branded policy transactions, premium, and commission data with annotations and reconciliation.
use_cases.pain_point: Policy premium, commission, and attribution data often live across multiple sources, so reconciling transactions and explaining discrepancies happens in spreadsheets.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate branded policy transactions, premium, and commission data, flag attribution gaps, let finance annotate corrections, and lock a reconciled view each period.

writeback_model.name: Attribution corrections
writeback_model.description: the documented fixes to mis-attributed policies or commissions

writeback_model.name: Reconciliation sign-offs
writeback_model.description: the record of who closed each premium reconciliation period

use_cases.title: Client Data Submission Portal
use_cases.card_summary: Give clients a governed portal to submit data directly instead of emailing spreadsheets back and forth.
use_cases.pain_point: Clients often submit data through spreadsheets, creating disconnected workflows and delayed responses with no real-time dialogue.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could give clients a governed interface to input and submit data directly, validate it on entry, and write it back to the warehouse, turning a stagnant exchange into a live workflow.

writeback_model.name: Client submissions
writeback_model.description: the data clients enter and submit directly through the portal

writeback_model.name: Validation flags
writeback_model.description: the rule checks and exceptions captured on each submission

use_cases.title: Funnel Failure Bucket Triage

value_stat.lbl: of quotes lost to fixable failures

use_cases.card_summary: Triage decline and failure buckets in the funnel, document root cause, and assign fixes to recover binds.
use_cases.pain_point: Decline reasons and failure buckets in the funnel are often reviewed in aggregate, so the specific, fixable causes of lost quotes aren't triaged or assigned.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface decline and failure buckets by type, let teams document root cause, assign fixes, and track recovered binds to outcome.

writeback_model.name: Failure root causes
writeback_model.description: the documented cause behind each material decline or failure bucket

writeback_model.name: Fix assignments
writeback_model.description: the owner and status of each remediation

use_cases.title: Reinsurance & Bookroll Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Track bookrolled policies and reinsurance positions against premium data with documented reconciliation.
use_cases.pain_point: Bookrolled policies and reinsurance positions are often tracked alongside premium data in spreadsheets, so reconciliation and exception handling lack a governed trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track bookrolled policies and reinsurance positions against premium data, flag mismatches, capture reconciliation notes, and report the current position with an audit trail.

writeback_model.name: Reconciliation notes
writeback_model.description: the documented disposition for each bookroll or reinsurance exception

writeback_model.name: Position adjustments
writeback_model.description: manual corrections to reinsurance or bookroll positions with rationale

use_cases.title: Outlier Agent Review Queue
use_cases.card_summary: Surface outlier agents and users in the funnel, document review, and route notification or coaching actions.
use_cases.pain_point: Outlier agents and anomalous user behavior in the funnel are often spotted in reports, but the review and follow-up actions live in email with no shared record.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface outlier agents and users, let teams document review decisions, trigger agent notifications via API action, and track coaching outcomes.

writeback_model.name: Review decisions
writeback_model.description: the documented disposition for each flagged agent or user

writeback_model.name: Action tracking
writeback_model.description: the notification or coaching action taken and its outcome

use_cases.title: Invoice & Standard Type Review
use_cases.card_summary: Categorize invoices against standard types, annotate anomalies, and feed clean revenue analysis.
use_cases.pain_point: Invoice categorization against standard types is often a manual reconciliation step, so miscategorized invoices distort revenue analysis until someone catches them.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface invoices against standard type dimensions, flag likely miscategorizations, let finance annotate and approve corrections, and feed a clean view into revenue analysis.

writeback_model.name: Categorization corrections
writeback_model.description: the documented recategorization of each flagged invoice

writeback_model.name: Approval record
writeback_model.description: who approved each recategorization and when

use_cases.title: In-Force Policy Attribution Audit
use_cases.card_summary: Audit in-force policies for correct attribution, document proof, and resolve disputed assignments.
use_cases.pain_point: Proof of attribution for in-force policies is often assembled reactively from multiple sources, so disputed assignments are hard to resolve with a clear record.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface in-force policies with their attribution, flag disputes, let teams document proof of attribution, and lock an audited record for each policy.

writeback_model.name: Attribution evidence
writeback_model.description: the documented proof supporting each policy's attribution

writeback_model.name: Dispute resolutions
writeback_model.description: the final attribution decision and approver for each disputed policy

workflows.industry_context: Insurance carriers and brokers run document-heavy underwriting, claims, and carrier-statement workflows that span submission intake, loss-run review, and financial reconciliation. A great deal of this work, including submission tracking, statement processing, and review collection, still happens through CSV exchanges, spreadsheets distributed many ways, and email, where status, comments, and audit trails are fragile.

use_cases.title: Underwriting Submission Pipeline
use_cases.card_summary: Upload submissions, track status through underwriting, capture comments, and analyze conversion by broker.
use_cases.pain_point: Underwriting submissions are often tracked in spreadsheets and email, so status, comments, and conversion by broker are hard to see, and submissions stall unnoticed.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let underwriters upload submission data, track status through stages with dropdowns, capture comments, and analyze conversion ratios by broker and underwriter.

writeback_model.name: Submission status
writeback_model.description: the current stage and disposition of each submission

writeback_model.name: Underwriter comments
writeback_model.description: the notes and decisions captured against each submission

use_cases.title: Loss Run Claims Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Ingest loss runs, structure claims data, document review, and feed underwriting decisions in one workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Loss runs arrive as documents and get re-keyed for review, so claims history that should inform underwriting is slow to structure and easy to mis-transcribe.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could ingest loss-run documents, use AI to extract claims data, let underwriters review and annotate, flag adverse history, and feed the result into the underwriting decision.

writeback_model.name: Claims annotations
writeback_model.description: the underwriter notes and adverse-history flags per loss run

writeback_model.name: Review decisions
writeback_model.description: the documented underwriting impact of each loss-run review

use_cases.title: Carrier Statement Reconciliation
use_cases.card_summary: Process carrier statements, reconcile against submissions, and resolve commission and premium discrepancies.
use_cases.pain_point: Carrier statements are often processed and reconciled against internal records in spreadsheets, so commission and premium mismatches surface late and lack a clear trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could process carrier statements, reconcile lines against submissions and policies with fallback matching, flag discrepancies, and capture documented resolutions.

writeback_model.name: Discrepancy resolutions
writeback_model.description: the documented disposition for each mismatched statement line

writeback_model.name: Reconciliation sign-offs
writeback_model.description: the record of who closed each statement reconciliation

use_cases.title: Lead Buyer CPA Optimization
use_cases.card_summary: Input conversion data against lead and call data to calculate CPA by buyer and optimize lead spend.
use_cases.pain_point: Lead and call performance is often analyzed by stitching conversion data into spreadsheets, so cost-per-acquisition by buyer and pricing decisions lack a single, repeatable view.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine lead and call data with user-entered conversion metrics, calculate CPA by buyer, and surface performance to optimize lead relationships and pricing.

writeback_model.name: Conversion inputs
writeback_model.description: the conversion metrics users enter per buyer and channel

writeback_model.name: Spend/pricing decisions
writeback_model.description: the documented changes to lead spend or pricing per buyer

use_cases.title: Distributed Review Collection
use_cases.card_summary: Replace splitting and re-collecting Excel files with a single secured workspace for structured input collection.
use_cases.pain_point: Collecting structured input across teams often means splitting an Excel sheet many ways, sending it out, and manually consolidating free-form responses with no access control.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could give each team a secured, row-level workspace to enter structured responses directly, eliminating spreadsheet distribution and manual consolidation.

writeback_model.name: Structured responses
writeback_model.description: the entries each team submits directly into the governed workspace

writeback_model.name: Submission status
writeback_model.description: which teams have completed input and which are outstanding

use_cases.title: Broker Performance Action Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Pair broker submission and conversion metrics with relationship-manager commentary and committed actions.
use_cases.pain_point: Broker reviews often produce conversion dashboards with no structured place to capture what relationship managers commit to do, so follow-through on key brokers is invisible.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit alongside broker performance, let relationship managers log commentary and committed actions on top brokers, and track them to the next review.

writeback_model.name: Action commitments
writeback_model.description: what each manager committed to for a broker, the owner, and due date

writeback_model.name: Broker commentary
writeback_model.description: root-cause notes attached to each broker's performance

use_cases.title: Submission Conversion Analysis
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze submission counts and conversion by broker, underwriter, and inception month, with annotated insights.
use_cases.pain_point: Conversion analysis by broker, underwriter, and inception month is often rebuilt in spreadsheets each period, so the 'why' behind conversion shifts isn't retained.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface submission counts and conversion by broker, underwriter, and inception month, flag shifts, and let teams annotate the cause for a retained, comparable view.

writeback_model.name: Conversion annotations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause behind each material conversion shift

use_cases.title: Claims Reserve Adjustment & Sign-Off
use_cases.card_summary: Capture case-reserve adjustments with documented rationale and management sign-off in an auditable workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Case-reserve adjustments are often made in spreadsheets and approved over email, leaving examiners without a clear trail from claim facts to the booked reserve.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could present open claims and current reserves, let adjusters enter changes with rationale, route management sign-off, and lock an auditable reserve history.

writeback_model.name: Reserve adjustments
writeback_model.description: each case-reserve change, the amount, and the supporting rationale

writeback_model.name: Sign-off record
writeback_model.description: who approved each reserve adjustment and when

use_cases.title: Submission Document Intake & Validation
use_cases.card_summary: Upload submission packages, auto-extract key data, and track missing items to a complete file.
use_cases.pain_point: Submission completeness is often tracked on a checklist in a shared drive, so missing applications or supporting documents surface late and stall quoting.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could ingest uploaded submission packages, use AI to extract key fields, flag missing items against a checklist, and track follow-ups to a complete file.

writeback_model.name: Checklist status
writeback_model.description: which required items are present, missing, or pending per submission

writeback_model.name: Follow-up assignments
writeback_model.description: who owns chasing each missing item and the due date

use_cases.title: Position Designation Data Collection
use_cases.card_summary: Collect position designations through a single controlled workspace with enforced input formats and security.
use_cases.pain_point: Position designations are often collected by sending multiple Excel files, creating challenges consolidating free-form text and enforcing consistent formats.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could provide a single managed platform with security controls and enforced formats where teams enter position designations directly, replacing file distribution.

writeback_model.name: Designation entries
writeback_model.description: the structured position designations each team submits

writeback_model.name: Validation status
writeback_model.description: which entries pass format rules and which need correction

workflows.industry_context: Private equity and venture firms run portfolio valuation, financial-statement analysis, and planning work that spans many portfolio entities, funds, and data sources. A large share of opex planning, budgeting, valuation, and data reconciliation still lives in co-authored Office 365 spreadsheets and hub-and-spoke Excel templates, where version control, audit trails, and transparency are weak.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Valuation & Sign-Off
use_cases.card_summary: Capture portfolio valuations and marks against benchmarks and market data with documented rationale and sign-off.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms in this sector often build portfolio valuations in spreadsheets, blending performance data with manual marks, leaving auditors without a clear trail from inputs to the booked valuation.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could present holdings with performance and market data, let analysts enter marks with documented methodology, route committee review, and lock an auditable valuation each period.

writeback_model.name: Valuation marks
writeback_model.description: the entered mark and supporting methodology for each holding

writeback_model.name: Committee sign-offs
writeback_model.description: the record of valuation approval by period and approver

use_cases.title: Opex Planning & Forecasting
use_cases.card_summary: Replace co-authored spreadsheets with a governed opex planning and forecasting workflow with full audit trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Division heads often plan independently in co-authored Office 365 spreadsheets, with manual adjustments to business data outside systems and no audit trail of who changed what.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could replace co-authored spreadsheets with a governed planning workflow where divisions enter opex plans and forecasts, capture rationale, and roll up to an enterprise view with full history.

writeback_model.name: Plan inputs
writeback_model.description: each division's opex plan and forecast adjustments by period

writeback_model.name: Approval and lock record
writeback_model.description: who approved and locked each planning cycle and when

use_cases.title: Excel-Replacement Budgeting Templates
use_cases.card_summary: Standardize localized and enterprise budgeting templates in one governed app for domain specialists.
use_cases.pain_point: Budgeting often relies on different finance and business-manager templates for localized versus enterprise processes, so consolidation is manual and standards drift across the hub-and-spoke model.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could publish standardized budgeting templates, let domain specialists enter data directly, validate against finance standards, and consolidate localized and enterprise budgets cleanly.

writeback_model.name: Budget entries
writeback_model.description: the data each domain specialist enters into the standardized template

writeback_model.name: Consolidation record
writeback_model.description: how localized inputs roll into the enterprise budget

use_cases.title: Financial Statement Variance Review
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze income-statement and balance-sheet variances across entities with annotated explanations and forecasting.
use_cases.pain_point: Income-statement and balance-sheet analysis is often done in spreadsheets across dev and prod sources, so variance explanations and forecasts live in side comments rather than with the numbers.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine income-statement and balance-sheet data, surface variances, let finance annotate explanations and apply forecast adjustments, and publish a collaborative review.

writeback_model.name: Variance explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of each material statement movement

writeback_model.name: Forecast adjustments
writeback_model.description: the interactive forecast changes applied during review

use_cases.title: Monthly KPI Commentary & Sign-Off
use_cases.card_summary: Pair monthly financial and operational KPIs with owner commentary and dynamic narrative for the reporting pack.
use_cases.pain_point: Monthly KPI packs across segments are often produced as dashboards while the narrative and commentary get rebuilt each cycle in email and slides.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface monthly KPIs across segments, flag movements, let owners add commentary and dynamic titles, and assemble a reusable reporting pack.

writeback_model.name: KPI commentary
writeback_model.description: the owner explanation attached to each material KPI movement

writeback_model.name: Narrative elements
writeback_model.description: the dynamic titles and notes that assemble the reporting pack

use_cases.title: Entity ID Review & Matching
use_cases.card_summary: Review and match entity identifiers across data sources, with manual matching where automation fails.
use_cases.pain_point: Matching entity and security identifiers across data sources is often a manual reconciliation, so when automated matching fails, the fixes happen in spreadsheets with no quality trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface automated ID-match results, let analysts manually match the records that fail, confirm decisions, and write resolved identifiers back to maintain data quality.

writeback_model.name: Manual matches
writeback_model.description: the analyst-confirmed matches for records automation could not resolve

writeback_model.name: Match audit log
writeback_model.description: the record of who matched each identifier and when

use_cases.title: Pipeline & Deal Tracking Workspace
use_cases.card_summary: Track deal pipeline stages, diligence tasks, and committee decisions in one governed workflow instead of spreadsheets.
use_cases.pain_point: Deal pipeline and diligence are often managed in separate spreadsheets per team, creating version-control and transparency issues across the pipeline.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track deals through pipeline stages, manage diligence tasks, capture committee decisions, and roll up a transparent pipeline view across teams.

writeback_model.name: Deal stage updates
writeback_model.description: the current stage and status of each deal in the pipeline

writeback_model.name: Committee decisions
writeback_model.description: the documented go/no-go decision and rationale per deal

use_cases.title: Data Reconciliation Sign-Off
use_cases.card_summary: Reconcile data across sources at close, document breaks, and capture sign-off in a governed workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Cross-source data reconciliation at close is often a manual spreadsheet exercise, so breaks and their explanations sit outside the books with no sign-off trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface reconciliation deltas across sources, let teams document break explanations and dispositions, and capture sign-off with a full history.

writeback_model.name: Break explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of each reconciliation difference

writeback_model.name: Reconciliation sign-offs
writeback_model.description: the record of who closed each reconciliation period

use_cases.title: Portfolio Company KPI Collection
use_cases.card_summary: Let portfolio companies submit KPIs directly into a governed workspace instead of emailing spreadsheets.
use_cases.pain_point: Portfolio company KPIs are often collected by emailing templates to each company and re-keying responses, so collection is slow and the data quality varies.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let portfolio companies enter KPIs directly into a secured, row-level workspace, validate on entry, and consolidate a clean portfolio-wide view.

writeback_model.name: Portco KPI submissions
writeback_model.description: the metrics each portfolio company enters directly

writeback_model.name: Submission status
writeback_model.description: which companies have reported and which are outstanding

use_cases.title: Capital Call & Distribution Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Track capital calls and distributions by LP and fund, document approvals, and reconcile against cash.
use_cases.pain_point: Capital calls and distributions are often administered in spreadsheets per fund, so LP allocations, approvals, and cash reconciliation lack a single governed record.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track capital calls and distributions by LP and fund, calculate allocations, capture approvals, and reconcile against cash with a full record.

writeback_model.name: Call/distribution records
writeback_model.description: the calculated and approved amount per LP and fund per event

writeback_model.name: Approval log
writeback_model.description: who approved each call or distribution and when

workflows.industry_context: Wealth and advisory firms run fund operations, NAV and distribution management, and SLA-driven servicing across retirement-plan and investment data. A great deal of distribution planning, operational commentary, SLA target setting, and manual operational tracking still lives in Excel models and spreadsheets outside the centralized data environment, where governance and audit trails are thin.

use_cases.title: Fund Distribution & NAV Planning

workflows.industry_context: Healthcare financial-infrastructure companies turn claims, eligibility, and care-coordination data into savings for customers, and increasingly want the write-back and inputting of numbers to happen in the BI layer rather than shuttling data between systems and spreadsheets. Compliance auditing and forecast scenarios are recurring multi-player, audit-sensitive workflows.

use_cases.title: Claims Savings Input Workflow

value_stat.lbl: of claims spend addressable for savings

use_cases.card_summary: Let customers enter and analyze claims and eligibility data to surface and track savings opportunities.
use_cases.pain_point: Companies providing claims insights often lack an efficient input system, so customers can't easily enter new claims information or track the savings opportunities surfaced from their data.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let customers input and analyze claims and eligibility data in input tables, surface savings opportunities, track disposition, and write results back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Claims inputs
writeback_model.description: the new claims and eligibility detail customers enter

writeback_model.name: Savings opportunities
writeback_model.description: each identified opportunity, its status, and the realized amount

use_cases.title: Quarterly Leave Compliance Audit
use_cases.card_summary: Log and categorize leave-case errors by law, severity, and root cause directly in a live audit workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Compliance teams often run quarterly leave audits by uploading CSVs and tracking errors in spreadsheets, with no live, writeable place to categorize errors by law, severity, and root cause.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could ingest quarterly audit uploads, let auditors categorize errors and score severity in input tables, and surface compliance trends across leave laws, managers, and companies.

writeback_model.name: Error categorizations
writeback_model.description: the error category, law, and severity score for each audited case

writeback_model.name: Audit reference data
writeback_model.description: the alias lookups and pivot data auditors maintain for the audit

use_cases.title: Forecast Scenario Write-Back
use_cases.card_summary: Let users create and manage multiple forecast scenarios and write new forecast data back to the warehouse.
use_cases.pain_point: Planners often need to create multiple forecast scenarios but data has to move in and out of systems and spreadsheets, with no way to input numbers and write back directly in the BI layer.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let users build forecast scenarios, input new figures in input tables, compare them, and write the chosen scenario back to the warehouse — bringing inputting and write-back into the BI layer.

writeback_model.name: Scenario inputs
writeback_model.description: the forecast figures entered for each scenario

writeback_model.name: Selected forecast
writeback_model.description: the scenario chosen and written back per planning cycle

workflows.industry_context: Gaming and wagering operators run dense risk, marketing, and player-management work between their gaming platform, affiliate systems, and CRM. steps: Load budget vs actual by channel

workflows.industry_context: Sports clubs and leagues run ticket-sales activity tracking, fan and member analytics, and affiliate or program management across CRM, dashboard tools, and spreadsheets. workflows.industry_context: Restaurant and hospitality tech providers run customer success, support, and growth-marketing motions across CRM, HubSpot, support systems, and enrichment data. workflows.industry_context: Multi-location restaurant and QSR chains run a dense layer of store-level accounting, financial close, and operations work between their POS, ERP, and head office.
