use_cases.title: Open Sales Order Exception Review
use_cases.card_summary: Review open sales-order exceptions across platforms, capture resolution notes, and write disposition back.
use_cases.pain_point: Open-order exceptions are often reviewed across COPA, sales-order, and material data in reports, with resolution notes captured in side spreadsheets disconnected from the order.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface open-order exceptions from integrated sales and material data, let customer service capture resolution notes, and write disposition straight back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Resolution notes
writeback_model.description: the disposition and findings per flagged order

writeback_model.name: Status updates
writeback_model.description: the current state of each exception and who owns it

workflows.industry_context: Industrial equipment makers run engineer-to-order project work, shop-floor production, and pipeline forecasting across Primavera, Agile PLM, ERP, and CRM. Teams stretch spreadsheets and shared files into makeshift apps to manage engineering backlogs, shop-floor status, change orders, and opportunity annotations, with weak traceability of updates.

use_cases.title: Engineering Backlog & On-Time Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Manage engineering project backlog, track on-time delivery by phase, and capture management comments and assignments.
use_cases.pain_point: Engineering teams in this sector often manage project backlog and on-time delivery across Primavera and spreadsheets, so task progress, resource assignments, and management comments live in disconnected places.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate project schedule data with custom backlog management, track on-time delivery by phase, and let engineering update status, add comments, and manage assignments in one place.

writeback_model.name: Backlog updates
writeback_model.description: task status, progress, and resource assignment per engineering item

writeback_model.name: Management comments
writeback_model.description: the notes and decisions attached to each project's status

use_cases.title: Shop Floor Status & QA Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Track planned vs actual hours, manage QA records, and coordinate fabrication releases across the production lifecycle.
use_cases.pain_point: Shop-floor status, quality records, and fabrication releases are often updated across disconnected systems, so production teams lack a real-time view of planned vs actual and QA status.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate manufacturing data with interactive elements for tracking planned vs actual hours, QA workflows, kit management, and fabrication releases, with real-time status updates.

writeback_model.name: Production status
writeback_model.description: the actual hours, stage, and status per shop-floor job

writeback_model.name: QA records
writeback_model.description: the quality result and disposition per inspected item

use_cases.title: Engineering Change Order Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Manage engineering change orders against P6 schedules and PLM, with impact review and approval.
use_cases.pain_point: Change orders on engineer-to-order machinery are often tracked in spreadsheets against the schedule, so cost and schedule impact surfaces late and approvals lack a trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could intake change orders, assess impact against P6 and PLM, route approval, and track the change to its schedule and cost effect on the project.

writeback_model.name: Change orders
writeback_model.description: the requested change, impact assessment, and cost per order

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each change order and when

use_cases.title: Pipeline Forecast & Backlog Look-Ahead
use_cases.card_summary: Forecast pipeline and manage backlog over a short look-ahead, blending project and opportunity data with user input.
use_cases.pain_point: Sales teams in this sector often forecast pipeline and backlog over a short horizon in spreadsheets, with no dynamic way to blend project and opportunity data or capture their inputs.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine project and opportunity data, let sales enter forecast inputs over a defined look-ahead, and produce a dynamic pipeline and backlog view.

writeback_model.name: Forecast inputs
writeback_model.description: the sales-entered pipeline and backlog estimates per period

writeback_model.name: Look-ahead notes
writeback_model.description: the context and assumptions behind each forecast

use_cases.title: Opportunity Annotation & Flagging
use_cases.card_summary: Annotate, update, and flag project and opportunity records with write-back directly to the warehouse.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector often track project and sales opportunities across CRM and project systems, with annotations and flags captured in side spreadsheets disconnected from the record.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface joined project and opportunity data, let teams annotate, update, or flag records via an input table, and write changes straight back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Opportunity annotations
writeback_model.description: the notes and flags added to each project or opportunity

writeback_model.name: Status updates
writeback_model.description: the current stage and owner per record

use_cases.title: Error Code & Grouping Override Manager
use_cases.card_summary: Manage error-code groupings and overrides in real time, replacing shared spreadsheets prone to duplicates.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector often manage changing groupings and error-code overrides through shared spreadsheets, producing duplicates and delayed updates.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let teams manage and update error codes and groupings in real time through input tables, eliminating duplicates and the lag of spreadsheet-based overrides.

writeback_model.name: Code overrides
writeback_model.description: the override or regrouping applied with reason

writeback_model.name: Change log
writeback_model.description: who changed each grouping and when

use_cases.title: Equipment Notes & Outlier Log
use_cases.card_summary: Capture notes on equipment rentals and outliers in a structured workflow, replacing clunky spreadsheets.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector often need to track notes about equipment rentals and outliers but rely on clunky spreadsheets, so context is lost and outliers go unexplained.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let teams capture equipment and rental notes and flag outliers in a structured input workflow, attach evidence, and write context straight back to the record.

writeback_model.name: Equipment notes
writeback_model.description: the structured note and outlier flag per asset or rental

writeback_model.name: Outlier explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause behind each flagged anomaly

use_cases.title: Material Shortage & Expedite Queue

value_stat.lbl: stall builds and slip ship dates

use_cases.card_summary: Surface part shortages against the build, capture expedite actions, and track resolution to keep jobs moving.
use_cases.pain_point: Material shortages against engineered builds are often tracked in spreadsheets and chased over email, so jobs stall and expedite actions go untracked.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface shortages against the build schedule, let production control capture expedite actions, and track each shortage to receipt with job-risk visibility.

writeback_model.name: Expedite actions
writeback_model.description: the action taken per shortage, owner, and expected date

writeback_model.name: Shortage status
writeback_model.description: the current state and job impact of each open shortage

use_cases.title: Quote-to-Order Margin Review
use_cases.card_summary: Review engineered quotes for margin and risk, capture pricing decisions, and route approval before order.
use_cases.pain_point: Engineered quotes are often priced in spreadsheets with margin and risk assumptions buried, so leadership can't see or govern how pricing decisions get made.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface quote cost and margin, flag low-margin or high-risk quotes, let estimators document rationale, and route approval before the order is locked.

writeback_model.name: Pricing rationale
writeback_model.description: the documented basis for each quote's margin and risk

writeback_model.name: Quote approvals
writeback_model.description: who approved each engineered quote and the conditions

use_cases.title: Document Management & Shortage Report
use_cases.card_summary: Manage engineering documents and shortage reports tied to project tasks, with status visible across teams.
use_cases.pain_point: Project documents and shortage reports are often tracked across PLM and shared drives, so document status and assignments are unclear and surface late in the schedule.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could tie engineering documents and shortage reports to project tasks, track status and assignment, flag overdue items, and roll up to project status.

writeback_model.name: Document status
writeback_model.description: the state and owner of each document tied to a task

writeback_model.name: Shortage entries
writeback_model.description: the reported shortage, task impact, and disposition

workflows.industry_context: Smart-manufacturing teams blend high-volume sensor and machine data in the warehouse with human knowledge that lives in operators' heads, spreadsheets, and disconnected entry tools. The recurring gap is enabling people to enter operational data that other applications can consume, and tying machine signals to maintenance, downtime, and quality actions that get tracked to outcome.

use_cases.title: Cross-App Operational Data Entry
use_cases.card_summary: Let people enter operational data once and write it back for other applications to consume across the data platform.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector repeatedly struggle to enable people to enter data that other applications can consume, lacking an efficient entry mechanism that integrates with their broader platform.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could provide the data-entry workflow teams lack, letting people enter operational data via input tables that write back to the warehouse for other applications to consume.

writeback_model.name: Operational entries
writeback_model.description: the records people create for downstream applications to read

writeback_model.name: Entry audit log
writeback_model.description: who entered or changed each record and when

use_cases.title: Downtime Event & Cause Capture
use_cases.card_summary: Log unplanned downtime, capture operator root-cause, and tie events to lost output unioned with machine data.
use_cases.pain_point: Organizations in this sector often capture machine signals automatically but rely on operators to explain downtime in their heads or on paper, so the why behind lost output is never structured.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface downtime events from machine data, let operators log structured root cause, and tie each event to estimated lost output for reliability analysis.

writeback_model.name: Downtime causes
writeback_model.description: the operator-entered category and root cause per event

writeback_model.name: Lost-output estimates
writeback_model.description: the duration and estimated lost production per event

use_cases.title: OEE Review & Improvement Actions
use_cases.card_summary: Pair OEE metrics by asset with operator commentary and committed improvement actions tracked to outcome.
use_cases.pain_point: OEE is often reported as a dashboard with the availability, performance, and quality losses behind it unexplained and no place to commit improvement actions.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit beside OEE metrics, break down loss categories, let operators log causes and committed actions, and track them to the next review.

writeback_model.name: Loss explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause behind each availability, performance, or quality loss

writeback_model.name: Improvement actions
writeback_model.description: the committed action, owner, and target per asset

use_cases.title: Predictive Maintenance Work Queue
use_cases.card_summary: Turn machine-health alerts into a work queue with technician disposition, action, and outcome tracking.
use_cases.pain_point: Machine-health alerts often arrive without a workflow, so technicians triage them ad hoc and the outcome of each predicted failure isn't captured to improve the model.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could turn machine-health signals into a triaged work queue, let technicians capture the action and outcome, and feed results back to improve predictions.

writeback_model.name: Alert dispositions
writeback_model.description: the triage decision and assigned technician per alert

writeback_model.name: Action outcomes
writeback_model.description: what was done and whether the predicted failure was avoided

use_cases.title: Tribal Knowledge Capture Log

value_stat.lbl: veterans take fix knowledge with them

use_cases.card_summary: Capture how veteran technicians diagnose and fix assets, tied to fault codes, before the knowledge walks out.
use_cases.pain_point: Veteran technicians in this sector are retiring and taking years of asset-fix knowledge with them, leaving fixes undocumented and tied only to individuals.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let technicians document symptoms, diagnosis, and fixes against fault codes, attach evidence, and build a searchable knowledge base tied to the assets.

writeback_model.name: Fix records
writeback_model.description: the symptom, diagnosis, and resolution per fault code or asset

writeback_model.name: Validation status
writeback_model.description: whether each documented fix has been reviewed and approved

value_case.lagging_financial_kpi: Mean time to repair / training cost

use_cases.title: Quality Defect Tag & Trend
use_cases.card_summary: Tag defects from machine-vision or inspection, capture disposition, and surface emerging trends for action.
use_cases.pain_point: Defects flagged by inspection or machine vision are often dispositioned in separate tools, so disposition notes and emerging trends aren't connected to the production data.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface flagged defects, let inspectors disposition and tag them, and surface emerging trends that trigger containment or process action.

writeback_model.name: Defect dispositions
writeback_model.description: the decision and defect-type tag per flagged item

writeback_model.name: Trend flags
writeback_model.description: emerging defect patterns raised for engineering action

use_cases.title: Sensor Data Quality & Gap Log

value_stat.lbl: a top barrier to IIoT value

use_cases.card_summary: Flag missing or anomalous sensor feeds, capture owner notes, and track gaps to resolution.
use_cases.pain_point: Sensor feeds in this sector frequently drop or drift, but gaps are noticed ad hoc, so analytics quietly run on incomplete data with no record of what was wrong.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could flag missing or anomalous sensor feeds, let owners annotate the cause, assign fixes, and track each data gap to resolution.

writeback_model.name: Data-gap records
writeback_model.description: the affected feed, asset, and suspected cause per gap

writeback_model.name: Resolution status
writeback_model.description: the fix owner and current state per gap

value_case.lagging_financial_kpi: Cost of decisions on bad data

use_cases.title: Production Run Parameter Log
use_cases.card_summary: Record operator setpoint changes and run parameters alongside machine data to tie settings to outcomes.
use_cases.pain_point: Operator setpoint changes and run parameters are often unlogged, so when yield or quality shifts there's no record of what was changed to explain it.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let operators log setpoint changes and parameters, union them with machine data, and let teams analyze how settings drove yield and quality.

writeback_model.name: Setpoint changes
writeback_model.description: the parameter changed, value, and reason per run

writeback_model.name: Run notes
writeback_model.description: operator context attached to each production run

use_cases.title: Energy & Utilization Action Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Surface energy and utilization anomalies from machine data with owner annotations and savings actions.
use_cases.pain_point: Energy and utilization signals are often captured but not acted on, so anomalies and savings opportunities sit in dashboards without owned follow-up.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface energy and utilization anomalies from machine data, let owners annotate cause, propose savings actions, and track them to outcome.

writeback_model.name: Anomaly annotations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause behind each energy or utilization anomaly

writeback_model.name: Savings actions
writeback_model.description: the proposed action, owner, and expected savings

use_cases.title: Spare Parts & Reorder Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Track spare-parts consumption against maintenance, capture reorder decisions, and push orders via API.
use_cases.pain_point: Spare-parts levels and reorder decisions are often managed in spreadsheets disconnected from maintenance activity, so stockouts extend downtime and overstock ties up cash.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track spare-parts consumption against maintenance, flag items below reorder point, capture and approve reorder decisions, and push approved orders via API action.

writeback_model.name: Reorder decisions
writeback_model.description: the quantity and approval per part below reorder point

writeback_model.name: Consumption notes
writeback_model.description: context on unusual usage tied to maintenance events

workflows.industry_context: Discrete and materials manufacturers reconcile financial data across multiple enterprise systems (GL, invoicing, analytics) and manage order, project, and cost-estimation workflows. Recurring patterns are R&M and reconciliation exception response, budget and actuals entry that today round-trips through spreadsheets, and cost estimation with manual overrides and scenario analysis.

use_cases.title: Finance Reconciliation & R&M Response
use_cases.card_summary: Monitor and reconcile financial data across AP, AR, GL, and capex, and respond to R&M cost exceptions in input tables.
use_cases.pain_point: Finance teams in this sector reconcile data across accounts payable, receivable, GL, capex, and repair-and-maintenance, with exception responses captured in spreadsheets disconnected from the numbers.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate financial data across systems, flag repair-and-maintenance exceptions, and let finance respond directly in input tables alongside the production data.

writeback_model.name: Exception responses
writeback_model.description: the documented response and disposition per flagged R&M or cost exception

writeback_model.name: Sign-off records
writeback_model.description: who reviewed each reconciliation and when

use_cases.title: Enterprise Financial Data Integration
use_cases.card_summary: Consolidate financial data across GL, invoice, and analytics systems into unified, annotated reporting.
use_cases.pain_point: Producers in this sector often pull financial data from multiple secure enterprise systems with no unified place to reconcile and annotate it, so reporting is slow and fragmented.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate master, GL, and invoice data from enterprise systems, let finance annotate variances, and publish unified financial reporting from one governed view.

writeback_model.name: Variance annotations
writeback_model.description: the documented explanation for each material movement

writeback_model.name: Mapping overrides
writeback_model.description: manual mappings applied to align systems for reporting

use_cases.title: Budget & Actuals Direct Entry
use_cases.card_summary: Let business users enter budget and actuals directly, writing to the warehouse without a spreadsheet intermediary.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector often enter budget numbers and actuals into spreadsheets before transferring to the warehouse, creating an inefficient multi-step process with sync risk.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let business users enter budget numbers and actuals directly into input tables that write to the warehouse, eliminating the spreadsheet intermediary.

writeback_model.name: Budget entries
writeback_model.description: the budget and actual figures entered by period and business unit

writeback_model.name: Entry history

use_cases.title: Mix Design & Cost Estimator
use_cases.card_summary: Calculate material costs, model mix designs, and run what-if scenarios with manual cost overrides.
use_cases.pain_point: Producers in this sector often estimate project costs and optimize material mixes in spreadsheets, with no live link to GL and material costs or a record of override assumptions.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate GL and material costs, let teams design mixes, enter manual cost overrides, and run scenario analysis with a governed estimate.

writeback_model.name: Cost overrides

writeback_model.name: Mix scenarios
writeback_model.description: the proposed mix designs and their modeled cost

use_cases.title: Construction Project & Job Cost Tracker

use_cases.pain_point: Project, cost, and milestone data are often spread across the construction-management system, GL, and field tracking, so job costing and milestone status surface late.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate project, GL, and field data to provide comprehensive job costing and milestone management, with field updates and at-risk flagging.

writeback_model.name: Milestone updates

writeback_model.name: Cost annotations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of cost variances per job

use_cases.title: Corporate Order Tracking & Analysis
use_cases.card_summary: Track corporate order status, analyze patterns, and capture fulfillment notes for data-driven decisions.
use_cases.pain_point: Order tracking and analysis are often done in reports with fulfillment notes and decisions captured separately, so order status and patterns aren't connected to action.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could provide order tracking and analysis, let teams annotate fulfillment issues, and capture decisions on order and customer relationships in one place.

writeback_model.name: Fulfillment notes

writeback_model.name: Order status
writeback_model.description: the current state and owner per order in question

use_cases.title: Marketing Goals Input & Tracking

use_cases.pain_point: Marketing teams in this sector often manage monthly goals in external sheets that must be synced over, creating unnecessary transfer steps and sync issues.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let marketing input and track monthly goals directly in the platform, eliminating the external sheet and its sync issues.

writeback_model.name: Goal entries
writeback_model.description: the monthly marketing goals entered by category

writeback_model.name: Actual tracking

use_cases.pain_point: Regional sales forecasting often blends order, run, and shipping data in reports while forecast anchors and assumptions live in side spreadsheets disconnected from the metrics.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate order, print-run, and shipping data, let analysts enter forecast anchors and segment inputs, and produce a regional forecast with full history.

use_cases.title: Plant R&M Cost Action Tracker

use_cases.pain_point: Plant repair-and-maintenance spend is often reviewed as a trend with no structured place to capture why it moved or what action owners committed to.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface R&M cost trends by plant, flag over-plan spend, let owners log cause and committed actions, and track them to outcome.

writeback_model.name: Cost explanations

writeback_model.name: Cost actions

use_cases.title: Capex Request & Approval Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Intake capital expenditure requests, route tiered approval, and track committed spend against plan.
use_cases.pain_point: Capex requests are often submitted over email and tracked in spreadsheets, so approvals lack a trail and committed spend drifts from plan.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could intake capex requests, check them against the capital plan, route tiered approval, and track committed spend against budget.

writeback_model.name: Capex requests
writeback_model.description: the requested project, justification, and amount

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each request, the tier, and the committed amount

workflows.industry_context: Field-services and facilities organizations coordinate multi-site operations, supplier and contract management, and field-workforce data across procurement, facilities, and operations systems. Recurring patterns are budget allocation and vehicle/repair data entry that today live in low-code tools, supplier and item harmonization, contract and inflation tracking, and exception management with row-level access by site or client.

use_cases.title: Budget Allocation & Writeback

value_stat.lbl: tools force complex dev for entry

use_cases.card_summary: Let user groups allocate budgets at granular levels with automatic calculations and direct writeback to the warehouse.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector often allocate budgets through low-code tools that force users to learn complex development for simple entry, with allocations re-keyed and disconnected from the warehouse.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let user groups input budget allocations at a granular level with automatic calculations and direct writeback, replacing the low-code tool.

writeback_model.name: Allocation entries
writeback_model.description: the budget split entered by user group at the lower granularity

writeback_model.name: Calculation overrides
writeback_model.description: manual adjustments to the auto-calculated allocation

use_cases.title: Vehicle & Repair Data Input

value_stat.lbl: separate system for field entry today

use_cases.card_summary: Let field teams update vehicle and repair fields with writeback in the same platform used for reporting.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams in this sector often manage field data input through a separate third-party system, and struggle to get people to enter the relevant vehicle and repair data at all.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could replace the separate input system with writeback in the same platform used for reporting, letting field teams update vehicle and repair fields directly.

writeback_model.name: Repair updates
writeback_model.description: the repair field values and status entered per vehicle

writeback_model.name: Entry log
writeback_model.description: who updated each record and when

use_cases.title: Vendor & Item Harmonization Review

use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface unharmonized item and vendor names, present AI-generated suggestions, let reviewers approve or deny through an interactive workflow, and maintain consistent naming.

writeback_model.name: Reviewer feedback

use_cases.title: Workforce Scheduling Scenario Planner

use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let planners model workforce coverage scenarios across sites, adjust assignments, capture the approved plan, and write it back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Schedule scenarios

writeback_model.name: Approved schedule

writeback_model.name: Supplier mappings

use_cases.title: Service Work Order & SLA Tracker

workflows.industry_context: Agribusiness and ag-biotech teams run dense breeding, field-trial, and lab workflows where decisions on which plants to keep, advance, or discard hinge on stitching together LIMS, molecular assay results, and field observations. Much of the data collection and annotation still happens in Monday.com, Excel, and disconnected tools, with weak governance over who changed what.

use_cases.title: Plant Breeding Selection Decisions
use_cases.card_summary: Track plant samples through molecular screening and let researchers record keep/discard decisions with rationale in one governed workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Breeding teams in this sector often pull genotype screening, morphology, and assay results from separate LIMS and lab systems, then make keep-or-discard calls in spreadsheets with no record of the reasoning.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate molecular assay and observation data, present each plant's screening status, and let researchers record keep/discard decisions with documented rationale and a full selection history.

writeback_model.name: Selection decisions
writeback_model.description: where researchers record keep, advance, or discard calls per plant with the supporting rationale

writeback_model.name: Reviewer confirmations
writeback_model.description: the record of who confirmed each cohort decision and when

use_cases.title: Farmer Field Data Collection

value_stat.lbl: data gathered across full crop year

use_cases.card_summary: Let growers enter field data through standardized forms that write straight back to the warehouse, replacing Monday.com and circulated spreadsheets.
use_cases.pain_point: Agribusinesses often gather grower field data across a season using Monday.com or emailed spreadsheets, forcing manual consolidation and leaving entries inconsistent and ungoverned.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could give growers standardized input forms with controlled dropdowns and validation, writing data directly to the warehouse and removing the manual file-circulation step.

writeback_model.name: Field observations
writeback_model.description: the grower-entered field data captured through standardized, validated forms

writeback_model.name: Entry corrections
writeback_model.description: edits and resubmissions with who changed each value

use_cases.title: Lab Inventory & Sample Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Track laboratory inventory and experiment samples through their lifecycle with location, status, and chain-of-custody capture.
use_cases.pain_point: Ag-biotech labs often track reagent inventory and sample movement on shared spreadsheets, so location and consumption fall out of sync and experiments stall waiting on materials.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let lab staff log samples and inventory, update status and location as work proceeds, flag low stock, and reconcile against the LIMS of record.

writeback_model.name: Inventory transactions
writeback_model.description: each receipt, move, or consumption event per sample or reagent

writeback_model.name: Custody updates
writeback_model.description: the current location and handler for each tracked item

use_cases.title: Gene-Edit Experiment Status Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Track gene-editing experiments from initiation through data ingestion, monitoring efficiency and regeneration across genotypes.
use_cases.pain_point: Editing programs often monitor experiment progress, insertion frequency, and regeneration rates across spreadsheets and lab notebooks, making program-wide status and efficiency hard to see.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track each editing experiment's status, capture efficiency and survival metrics by genotype and treatment, and roll up a live program view from initiation to data ingestion.

writeback_model.name: Experiment status
writeback_model.description: the current stage and outcome metrics for each editing experiment

writeback_model.name: Treatment notes
writeback_model.description: documented observations per genotype and treatment condition

use_cases.title: Lab-to-Field Trial Handoff
use_cases.card_summary: Manage the handoff of advanced lines from lab to field trials with checklists, approvals, and trial assignments.
use_cases.pain_point: The transition from lab experiment to field testing is frequently coordinated over email and spreadsheets, so line readiness, seed availability, and trial assignments drift and planting windows are missed.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface lines ready to advance, validate readiness against a checklist, assign them to trial sites, capture approval, and track each handoff to planting.

writeback_model.name: Handoff records
writeback_model.description: each line advanced to field with its trial assignment and readiness checklist

writeback_model.name: Handoff approvals
writeback_model.description: who approved each lab-to-field transition and when

use_cases.title: Metadata Annotation & Cleanup
use_cases.card_summary: Let users annotate and correct incomplete or wrong metadata directly, writing fixes back to the warehouse with an audit trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Research data in this sector is often incomplete, mislabeled, or missing metadata, and corrections happen in ad-hoc spreadsheets that never make it back to the source of record.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could flag records with incomplete metadata, let users enter corrections with validation, route review, and write the cleaned values straight back to the warehouse.

writeback_model.name: Metadata corrections
writeback_model.description: the corrected field values entered by users with the original value preserved

writeback_model.name: Approval log
writeback_model.description: who approved each correction and the timestamp

use_cases.title: Input Cost & Margin Planning
use_cases.card_summary: Plan seed, fertilizer, and chemical inputs against expected yield and price, capturing scenario assumptions for margin per field.
use_cases.pain_point: Input cost and margin planning is often modeled in spreadsheets per field, with assumptions emailed around and no scenario history to compare before committing to a season.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let agronomy and finance model input costs against expected yield and price by field, compare margin scenarios, and lock an approved plan with full history.

writeback_model.name: Planning assumptions
writeback_model.description: the yield, price, and input cost assumptions per field and scenario

writeback_model.name: Approved plans
writeback_model.description: the locked input and margin plan for the season

use_cases.title: Field Trial Protocol Compliance
use_cases.card_summary: Track field-trial protocol adherence, deviations, and sign-offs to keep regulated trials audit-ready.
use_cases.pain_point: Regulated field trials require documented protocol adherence, but deviations and approvals are often captured on paper or scattered files, leaving gaps when regulators or auditors review.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track each trial against its protocol, capture deviations with documented rationale, route sign-off, and assemble an audit-ready compliance package.

writeback_model.name: Deviation records
writeback_model.description: each protocol deviation, the reason, and corrective action

writeback_model.name: Sign-off log
writeback_model.description: who reviewed and approved each trial step and when

use_cases.title: Grower Contract & Acreage Intake
use_cases.card_summary: Intake grower contracts and committed acreage with document upload, validation, and approval to plan supply early.
use_cases.pain_point: Grower contracts and committed acreage are frequently collected on paper and re-keyed into spreadsheets, so supply visibility lags and contract terms aren't validated until late.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could ingest grower contracts, extract acreage and terms, validate them, capture approval, and roll up committed supply for early planning.

writeback_model.name: Contract enrollments
writeback_model.description: the captured acreage and terms for each grower contract

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each enrollment and any term exceptions

use_cases.title: Harvest & Yield Reconciliation

value_stat.lbl: gap between estimated and actual yield

use_cases.card_summary: Reconcile estimated versus actual harvest yield by field and capture variance explanations to improve next-season forecasts.
use_cases.pain_point: Harvest results are often compared to estimates in spreadsheets, so the reasons behind yield gaps go uncaptured and never feed back into forecasting.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could compare estimated and actual yield by field, surface variances, and capture root-cause annotations that sharpen the next season's forecast.

writeback_model.name: Variance explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of each material yield gap by field

workflows.industry_context: Commodity traders and AgTech providers run origination, contract management, logistics, and settlement across fragmented systems where a 1% moisture swing, a late truck, or a price move decides profit or loss. Critical workflows still ride on circulated Excel files, manual registers, and disconnected tools across regional offices, making consolidation slow and error-prone.

use_cases.title: Grain Contract Lifecycle Management

value_stat.lbl: moisture swing can flip a margin

use_cases.card_summary: Manage commodity contracts from creation through pricing, delivery, and settlement in one governed workflow instead of spreadsheets.
use_cases.pain_point: Trading firms in this sector often manage the full contract lifecycle across spreadsheets and disconnected tools, so pricing terms, delivery schedules, and settlement drift apart and errors hide until settlement.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could bring contract creation, pricing, delivery tracking, and settlement into one workflow, capturing amendments with approval and reconciling each contract to payment.

writeback_model.name: Contract terms
writeback_model.description: the pricing, quality, and delivery terms for each commodity contract

writeback_model.name: Amendments + settlements
writeback_model.description: each term change and final settlement decision with approver

use_cases.title: Regional Data Consolidation Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Let regional offices enter data directly into a governed workspace, replacing emailed Excel files and manual consolidation.
use_cases.pain_point: Organizations in this sector often send Excel files to many regional offices, who add rows and ship the files back for one person to consolidate, creating slow, error-prone reporting.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let each regional office enter data directly into a shared input table, replacing circulated Excel files with a single governed, real-time consolidation.

writeback_model.name: Regional submissions
writeback_model.description: the rows each office enters directly, replacing the emailed spreadsheets

writeback_model.name: Submission status
writeback_model.description: which offices have submitted, are pending, or need correction

use_cases.title: Position & Hedge Exposure Monitor
use_cases.card_summary: Track physical positions against hedges, flag unhedged exposure, and capture trader decisions with an audit trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Net positions and hedge coverage are frequently reconciled in spreadsheets after the close, leaving unhedged exposure invisible during the trading day.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could net physical positions against hedges, flag exposure outside policy, let traders log a documented decision, and track it through to hedge execution.

writeback_model.name: Exposure decisions
writeback_model.description: the trader's documented action on each flagged exposure

writeback_model.name: Hedge tracking
writeback_model.description: the status of each hedge from decision to execution

use_cases.title: Quality & Moisture Discount Capture
use_cases.card_summary: Capture inbound quality and moisture grades at intake and apply contract discount schedules consistently.
use_cases.pain_point: Quality grading and moisture discounts are often recorded on paper tickets and keyed in later, so discount schedules are applied inconsistently and revenue leaks at intake.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let intake staff record quality and moisture, auto-apply the contract discount schedule, confirm with the grower, and feed the adjustment to settlement.

writeback_model.name: Quality records
writeback_model.description: the captured grade and moisture per inbound load

writeback_model.name: Discount adjustments
writeback_model.description: the applied price adjustment and the schedule used

use_cases.title: Logistics & Freight Coordination

value_stat.lbl: many handoffs, each a risk point

use_cases.card_summary: Coordinate truck, rail, and vessel movements from farm to delivery with status updates and exception handling.
use_cases.pain_point: Moving commodities from farm to silo to vessel involves many handoffs tracked in registers and email, so a late truck or missed slot surfaces too late to fix.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could schedule movements, assign carriers, capture status updates and delay exceptions, and confirm delivery against contract windows.

writeback_model.name: Movement records
writeback_model.description: each scheduled movement with carrier, slot, and status

writeback_model.name: Exception log
writeback_model.description: delays and disruptions with cause and resolution

use_cases.title: Storage Settlement & Billing
use_cases.card_summary: Calculate storage charges by duration and volume, capture adjustments, and settle billing with grower confirmation.
use_cases.pain_point: Storage fees based on duration, volume, and rate are often calculated in spreadsheets, so charges are slow to bill and disputes lack a clear record.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could calculate storage charges from volume and duration, capture adjustments with rationale, confirm with the grower, and produce a settled bill.

writeback_model.name: Storage charges
writeback_model.description: the calculated fee per grower position with the rate basis

writeback_model.name: Adjustment notes
writeback_model.description: any negotiated adjustment and the reason

use_cases.title: Packaging Regulation Compliance
use_cases.card_summary: Guide clients through packaging regulation questions to assign correct compliance codes and save them straight to the data template.
use_cases.pain_point: Clients often answer packaging regulation questions in a separate web calculator and cannot save the resulting color or compliance codes back into their data templates, forcing manual re-entry.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let clients answer regulation questions, derive the correct component compliance codes, and write the result straight into their data-collection templates.

writeback_model.name: Compliance assignments
writeback_model.description: the derived regulation codes saved per component

writeback_model.name: Response history
writeback_model.description: the answers given and any revisions over time

use_cases.title: Counterparty Credit & Limit Review
use_cases.card_summary: Review counterparty credit, set exposure limits, and capture approvals so trading stays within credit policy.
use_cases.pain_point: Counterparty credit limits and exposure are frequently tracked in spreadsheets, so traders lack a live view of remaining limit and approvals are hard to evidence.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface counterparty exposure, let credit teams review and set limits with documented approval, and publish a live limit view to trading.

writeback_model.name: Credit limits
writeback_model.description: the approved exposure limit per counterparty and effective date

writeback_model.name: Review notes
writeback_model.description: the credit rationale and collateral basis for each decision

use_cases.title: Basis & Price Bid Sheet Control

value_stat.lbl: basis bids change with the market

use_cases.card_summary: Build and approve daily basis bid sheets by location before they are published to growers and posted.
use_cases.pain_point: Daily basis bids are often built in spreadsheets and emailed to locations to post, with no approval record or history of what was quoted where.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let merchandisers set basis bids against futures and freight, review margin impact, capture approval, and publish a controlled bid sheet with full history.

writeback_model.name: Bid proposals
writeback_model.description: the proposed basis bids by location with margin assumptions

writeback_model.name: Approved bid sheets
writeback_model.description: the published, approved bids for each effective period

use_cases.title: Trade Dispute & Claim Resolution

value_stat.lbl: faster claim resolution with a queue

use_cases.card_summary: Work quality, weight, and delivery claims through a governed queue with evidence upload and disposition tracking.
use_cases.pain_point: Quality, weight, and delivery disputes are often resolved over email with evidence in scattered folders, so claims stall and the resolution trail is incomplete.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could centralize claims, let staff attach evidence and document disposition, route approval, and feed the resolution to settlement.

writeback_model.name: Claim records
writeback_model.description: each dispute with type, counterparty, and disposition

writeback_model.name: Resolution approvals
writeback_model.description: who approved each claim outcome and the settled amount

workflows.industry_context: Forestry and timber operators manage harvest planning, log inventory, fleet logistics, and a complex chain of custody from forest to mill, where origin data is easily lost across multiple intermediaries. Teams rely on fragmented data and manual processes that struggle to keep pace with multi-season, multi-crew operations, and field telematics and financials rarely meet in one governed place.

use_cases.title: Fleet Utilization & Maintenance Control
use_cases.card_summary: Monitor vehicle utilization and maintenance against telematics, flag underperforming assets, and schedule service in one workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Forestry fleet managers often pull telematics, utilization, and maintenance data into separate reports, making it hard to see which assets are underused or overdue for service.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate vehicle telematics with maintenance records, surface utilization and service status by asset, and let managers schedule and track maintenance with a full history.

writeback_model.name: Service schedule
writeback_model.description: the planned and completed maintenance per asset with reason

writeback_model.name: Utilization notes
writeback_model.description: documented explanations for under- or over-used assets

use_cases.title: Log Load & Scale Ticket Capture

value_stat.lbl: every load is a settlement event

use_cases.card_summary: Capture digital load and scale ticket data across the forest-to-mill chain so owners, contractors, and mills share one record.
use_cases.pain_point: Load and scale data is often captured on paper tickets across the forest-to-mill supply chain, so forest owners, contractors, and mills work from different records and reconciliation is painful.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could capture load and scale data at each step, match loads to harvest units, and give owners, contractors, and mills a single shared, reconciled record.

writeback_model.name: Load tickets
writeback_model.description: the digital record of each load, weight, and harvest unit

writeback_model.name: Reconciliation status
writeback_model.description: matched vs disputed loads between owner and mill

use_cases.title: Branch Forecast & Variance Analysis
use_cases.card_summary: Let branch managers input revenue projections, capture variance reasons, and analyze performance against budget and forecast.
use_cases.pain_point: Branch-level forecasting and variance analysis is frequently done in spreadsheets, so projections, budgets, and the reasons behind variances live in disconnected files.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine historical orders, timecards, and budget data with manager-entered projections, generate forecasts, and capture variance drivers in dedicated input tables.

writeback_model.name: Projection inputs
writeback_model.description: the revenue projections each branch manager enters by period

writeback_model.name: Variance reasons
writeback_model.description: documented drivers behind each material budget-to-actual gap

use_cases.title: Harvest Unit Planning & Scheduling
use_cases.card_summary: Plan harvest units against crew, fleet, and transport variables over an evolving multi-season horizon.
use_cases.pain_point: Harvest planning juggles units, product levels, crew and fleet assignments, and transport costs over an evolving multi-season horizon, and fragmented spreadsheets can't keep pace.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let planners assign crews and fleet to harvest units, sequence operations across seasons, capture plan changes with rationale, and track plan against actual progress.

writeback_model.name: Harvest assignments
writeback_model.description: the crew, fleet, and timing assigned to each harvest unit

writeback_model.name: Plan revisions
writeback_model.description: documented changes to the harvest sequence and the reason

use_cases.title: Chain-of-Custody & Origin Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Track timber origin and chain of custody from forest to mill to prove legal, certified sourcing for due diligence.
use_cases.pain_point: Timber passes through multiple intermediaries and processing stages, so origin data is easily lost and firms lack verified forest-level visibility for legality and certification due diligence.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could register origin at harvest, capture each custody handoff, collect certification evidence uploads, and assemble a complete chain-of-custody record for due diligence.

writeback_model.name: Custody handoffs
writeback_model.description: each transfer of timber with origin, party, and timestamp

writeback_model.name: Certification evidence
writeback_model.description: uploaded proof of legal and certified sourcing per lot

use_cases.title: Timber Inventory Reclassification
use_cases.card_summary: Reclassify inventory between product or grade categories with write-back, validation, and an audit trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Reclassifying timber inventory between categories is often a manual, multi-tool process, leading to inefficient data management and miscategorization.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let staff reclassify inventory between categories with validation, capture notes and rationale, and write the change back to the warehouse with an audit trail.

writeback_model.name: Reclassification entries
writeback_model.description: each category change with the original and new value

writeback_model.name: Notes + audit trail
writeback_model.description: the rationale and who made each reclassification

use_cases.title: Project Time & Utilization Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Capture project hours by crew and feed utilization metrics, replacing missing centralized time tracking.
use_cases.pain_point: Operators in this sector often lack centralized time tracking, making it hard to capture project hours and report utilization across business units.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let crews enter project hours in input tables, validate them against active projects, and produce utilization metrics for downstream management systems.

writeback_model.name: Time entries
writeback_model.description: the hours each crew records by project and task

writeback_model.name: Approvals
writeback_model.description: the record of who approved each timesheet and when

use_cases.title: Stumpage & Timber Sale Bids
use_cases.card_summary: Manage timber sale and stumpage bids by tract with valuation assumptions, approval, and award tracking.
use_cases.pain_point: Stumpage and timber sale bids are frequently modeled in spreadsheets and emailed for approval, with no consistent valuation basis or record of what was bid and awarded.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let commercial teams model tract valuations, compare bid scenarios, capture approval, and track each bid through to award with full history.

writeback_model.name: Bid valuations
writeback_model.description: the assumptions and proposed bid per tract

writeback_model.name: Award tracking
writeback_model.description: the approved bid and award outcome per sale

use_cases.title: Timber Harvest Permit Compliance
use_cases.card_summary: Track timber harvesting plan permits, conditions, and inspection sign-offs to keep operations within approved terms.
use_cases.pain_point: Harvest permits carry conditions and inspection requirements that are often tracked on checklists in shared drives, so compliance status is unclear until an inspector arrives.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track each harvest permit's conditions, capture compliance evidence and inspection sign-offs, and show live permit status across operations.

writeback_model.name: Condition status
writeback_model.description: the compliance state of each permit condition per harvest

writeback_model.name: Inspection sign-offs
writeback_model.description: the record of who validated each condition and when

use_cases.title: Reforestation & Silviculture Tracking
use_cases.card_summary: Track replanting, survival surveys, and silviculture treatments by stand to manage long-cycle regeneration obligations.
use_cases.pain_point: Replanting, survival surveys, and treatment schedules are tracked across spreadsheets over many years, so regeneration obligations and follow-up treatments slip unnoticed.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track replanting, survival surveys, and treatment schedules by stand, capture field results, and roll up regeneration obligation status over the cycle.

writeback_model.name: Silviculture records
writeback_model.description: the planting, survey, and treatment events per stand

writeback_model.name: Obligation status
writeback_model.description: the regeneration commitment and progress per stand

workflows.industry_context: Mining and quarrying operators run production planning, material consumption, inventory, and equipment health across ERP, supply chain, and IoT telematics data. Engineers manually process large volumes of equipment alerts and reports, adding expert commentary by hand, while market and shipment performance analysis is stitched together from disconnected sources.

use_cases.title: Production Schedule & Material Flow
use_cases.card_summary: Plan production schedules, track material consumption, and optimize resource allocation across workcenters in one workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Operators in this sector often plan production, track material consumption, and balance workcenter utilization across ERP and supply chain exports, leaving schedules and material demand out of sync.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate production, inventory, and material data, let planners adjust schedules and resource allocation, and capture plan changes against live utilization.

writeback_model.name: Schedule changes
writeback_model.description: adjustments to production sequence and workcenter assignment with reason

writeback_model.name: Consumption notes
writeback_model.description: documented material consumption variances per run

use_cases.title: Equipment Alert & Case Management
use_cases.card_summary: Triage equipment alerts against IoT data, attach expert comments, and manage each case to a customer recommendation.
use_cases.pain_point: Engineers in this sector often process dozens of equipment reports a month, each taking hours, configuring assets individually and writing comments in disconnected tools.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface equipment alerts beside their IoT data, let engineers attach comments directly to data points, disposition each case, and generate a customer recommendation.

writeback_model.name: Alert comments
writeback_model.description: expert commentary attached to specific IoT data points

writeback_model.name: Case dispositions
writeback_model.description: the evaluation outcome and recommendation per alert

use_cases.title: Customer Report Builder & Sign-Off
use_cases.card_summary: Let experts add commentary to automated report templates and verify them before customer delivery.
use_cases.pain_point: Engineers often copy-paste data visualizations between systems to build customer reports, then lack a check step before reports go out.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could assemble report templates from integrated data, let experts add commentary, route a verification check, and finalize each report for customer delivery.

writeback_model.name: Report commentary
writeback_model.description: the expert analysis added to each report template

writeback_model.name: Verification sign-off
writeback_model.description: who checked and approved each report before delivery

use_cases.title: On-Time Ship & Backorder Monitor
use_cases.card_summary: Monitor on-time shipping, track customer backorders, and capture actions to improve fulfillment service levels.
use_cases.pain_point: Shipping performance and backorders are often analyzed in static reports, so delivery bottlenecks and the actions to fix them aren't tracked in one place.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could monitor on-time performance against target dates, flag at-risk orders, let owners log corrective actions, and track backorders to resolution.

writeback_model.name: Fulfillment actions
writeback_model.description: the committed action and owner for each at-risk order

writeback_model.name: Backorder status
writeback_model.description: the current state and resolution path per backorder

use_cases.title: Market Outlook & Demand Annotation
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze production output and predicted demand by region with analyst commentary to inform market planning.
use_cases.pain_point: Market output, geographic distribution, and demand predictions are often analyzed in static workbooks where the analyst's interpretation lives outside the numbers.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit beside market output and demand data, flag significant shifts, capture analyst commentary on the outlook, and publish a reusable market view.

writeback_model.name: Market commentary
writeback_model.description: the analyst's documented interpretation of each significant market shift

use_cases.title: Ore Grade & Stockpile Reconciliation

value_stat.lbl: variance between planned and actual grade

use_cases.card_summary: Reconcile planned versus actual ore grade and stockpile balances, capturing variance explanations by source.
use_cases.pain_point: Planned versus mined ore grade and stockpile balances are frequently reconciled in spreadsheets, so grade variance and its causes go undocumented and feed back into the plan slowly.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could reconcile planned and actual grade and stockpile balances, surface variance by source, and capture root-cause annotations that improve mine planning.

writeback_model.name: Reconciliation entries
writeback_model.description: the planned-vs-actual grade and balance per source

writeback_model.name: Variance explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented cause of each material grade or balance gap

use_cases.title: Inventory & Spare Parts Control

value_stat.lbl: of spend tied in spare parts

use_cases.card_summary: Track consumables and spare parts against projected demand and BoM, flag stockouts, and capture reorder approvals.
use_cases.pain_point: Spare parts and consumables are often tracked against bill-of-materials demand in spreadsheets, so stockouts halt equipment and overstock ties up capital.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could project parts demand from BoM and schedules, track on-hand stock, flag stockout risk, route reorder approval, and sync to ERP.

writeback_model.name: Reorder requests
writeback_model.description: each proposed reorder with quantity and justification

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who approved each reorder against budget and when

use_cases.title: Safety Incident & Corrective Actions
use_cases.card_summary: Log safety incidents, attach evidence, and track corrective actions to closure with an audit-ready trail.
use_cases.pain_point: Safety incidents and corrective actions are often recorded on paper or in scattered files, making it hard to prove timely follow-up to regulators.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could centralize incident reporting, collect evidence uploads, assign and track corrective actions, and produce an audit-ready safety record.

writeback_model.name: Incident records
writeback_model.description: each safety event with severity, location, and detail

writeback_model.name: Corrective actions
writeback_model.description: the assigned action, owner, and closure status per incident

use_cases.title: Haul Cycle & Downtime Logging

value_stat.lbl: of output lost to equipment downtime

use_cases.card_summary: Log haul cycles and equipment downtime against assets to connect maintenance to lost production.
use_cases.pain_point: Haul cycles and downtime events are frequently recorded on paper or makeshift apps, so it's hard to connect equipment availability to lost production.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let field crews log downtime and haul cycle data on mobile, capture root cause, and tie each event to estimated lost production.

writeback_model.name: Downtime events
writeback_model.description: the cause, duration, and lost output of each equipment stoppage

writeback_model.name: Haul cycle entries
writeback_model.description: the recorded cycle data per asset and shift

use_cases.title: Environmental & Permit Monitoring
use_cases.card_summary: Track environmental monitoring readings against permit limits, document exceedances, and capture remediation sign-off.
use_cases.pain_point: Environmental readings such as dust, water, and noise are often logged in spreadsheets against permit limits, so exceedances and remediation lack a clear, timely trail.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could capture monitoring readings against permit limits, flag exceedances, document remediation, and route reviewer sign-off for an audit-ready record.

writeback_model.name: Monitoring readings
writeback_model.description: the recorded environmental measurements per site and period

writeback_model.name: Remediation records
writeback_model.description: the documented response and sign-off for each exceedance

workflows.industry_context: Oilfield services firms run dense vendor and subcontractor onboarding, workforce certification, and compliance workflows across upstream and midstream operations, integrating CRM, NetSuite, LMS, and custom platforms. Much of the certification tracking, COI management, and payroll validation runs through input tables and spreadsheets where compliance gaps and revenue leakage are easy to miss.

use_cases.title: Subcontractor Onboarding & COI Tracking

value_stat.lbl: COIs must be current before work

use_cases.card_summary: Manage subcontracted worker onboarding, COI history, and work requirements so every worker is compliant before starting.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms in this sector often manage subcontractor onboarding, certificate-of-insurance history, and work requirements across spreadsheets, so non-compliant workers can slip onto site.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could track each subcontracted worker through onboarding, capture COI and requirement status, and confirm compliance before clearing them to begin work.

writeback_model.name: Onboarding records
writeback_model.description: each worker's requirements, status, and COI history

writeback_model.name: Compliance clearances

use_cases.title: Vendor Onboarding Lifecycle Workflow

workflows.industry_context: Accounting and audit firms run engagement work across workpapers, PBC checklists, and review notes that live in Excel, Word, and email, where version control and audit trails are weak. workflows.industry_context: Central government agencies run national programs under stringent compliance regimes such as Uniform Guidance and Single Audit, where grants managers spend disproportionate time on antiquated monitoring and reporting processes. use_cases.title: Period Locking & Close Control
use_cases.card_summary: Govern period-locking requests so finance controls when accounting periods can be modified or closed, with synchronized state.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms often manage period-locking through informal requests and email, leaving uncertainty over which periods are open for changes and who authorized reopening them.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let finance submit period lock/unlock requests in input tables, route them for controller approval, synchronize the locked state, and retain a full authorization log.

writeback_model.name: Lock requests
writeback_model.description: where users request to open or close a period with justification

writeback_model.name: Authorization log
writeback_model.description: who approved each lock change and the resulting period state

use_cases.title: Asset Performance Watch List
use_cases.card_summary: Flag underperforming assets, override risk status, and annotate positions in real time against live portfolio data.
use_cases.pain_point: Asset teams often track at-risk holdings and risk-status overrides in spreadsheets, with annotations disconnected from the live portfolio and no record of override rationale.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could visualize holdings by investment type and value, let teams flag watch/monitor assets and override risk status in input tables, and capture comments back into the live workflow.

writeback_model.name: Risk overrides
writeback_model.description: where teams set watch/monitor designations with rationale per asset

writeback_model.name: Review comments
writeback_model.description: position-level annotations tied to each portfolio review

use_cases.title: Solvency & Compliance Testing
use_cases.card_summary: Process trial balances, input adjustments, and calculate solvency ratios with historical tracking for regulatory compliance.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms often run solvency tests across entities by manually adjusting trial balances in spreadsheets, making it hard to reproduce calculations and evidence compliance over time.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could process trial-balance data with input tables for adjustments, calculate solvency ratios against thresholds, and retain quarterly results for audit and trend analysis.

writeback_model.name: Adjustments
writeback_model.description: where users record reconciliations feeding the solvency calculation

writeback_model.name: Test results
writeback_model.description: the computed ratios and pass/fail status archived per period

use_cases.title: Portfolio Marketing Engagement Insight
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze user behavior and interest events to surface marketing effectiveness across a real estate platform.
use_cases.pain_point: Marketing teams often analyze platform engagement and interest events across fragmented exports, making it hard to connect campaigns to property-level engagement.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine dimensional user data with interest-event facts to surface engagement and marketing effectiveness, letting teams tag high-intent cohorts for follow-up.

writeback_model.name: Cohort tags
writeback_model.description: where marketers label high-intent user segments for routing

writeback_model.name: Campaign notes
writeback_model.description: observations tied to engagement shifts per campaign

use_cases.title: All-Assets Register & Status
use_cases.card_summary: Maintain a governed register of all assets with status, location, and depreciation in one place instead of siloed files.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms often track asset status, location, and depreciation across disconnected spreadsheets, creating reconciliation gaps and stale records.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could consolidate asset data into a governed register, let teams update status and location in input tables, and flag discrepancies for finance review.

writeback_model.name: Asset updates
writeback_model.description: where teams record status, location, and depreciation changes

writeback_model.name: Discrepancy flags
writeback_model.description: items where register and source data disagree, awaiting review

use_cases.title: External Tax Audit Data Collection
use_cases.card_summary: Let external tax advisors submit announced-audit data through governed forms with entity-level access, replacing email collection.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms often collect tax-audit information from a hundred or more external advisors across regions through inefficient email, with no standardization or access control by entity.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could provide input forms for external tax advisors to submit audit data directly, with row-level security controlling access by entity and validation ensuring quality.

writeback_model.name: Audit submissions
writeback_model.description: where external advisors enter announced-audit details per entity

writeback_model.name: Validation status
writeback_model.description: the reviewed/accepted state of each external submission

workflows.industry_context: ConTech platforms and their construction customers run project financials, cost-to-complete forecasting, change orders, and field operations off BuildOps and CRM data landed in the warehouse. Much of the forecasting still happens in Excel macros, BI tools exports, and disconnected estimating tools, where projection inputs and WIP analysis lack a governed, writeback-capable workflow.

use_cases.title: Cost-to-Complete Forecasting
use_cases.card_summary: Track actuals against budget and let PMs input completion-cost assumptions for live profitability forecasting, replacing Excel macros.
use_cases.pain_point: Construction teams often forecast project profitability by pulling bills, invoices, change orders, and payroll into Excel macros and BI tools exports, with projection inputs disconnected from live actuals.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine actual costs from bills, invoices, change orders, and payroll with PM-entered completion assumptions in input tables, producing cost-at-completion and projected profitability per project and period.

writeback_model.name: Cost-to-complete inputs
writeback_model.description: where PMs enter remaining-cost assumptions by cost code

writeback_model.name: Forecast versions
writeback_model.description: the projected profit and cost-at-completion saved per period

use_cases.title: WIP & Billing Status Review
use_cases.card_summary: Review work-in-progress, billing status, and budget variance from BuildOps data with input for projection assumptions.
use_cases.pain_point: Finance teams often assemble work-in-progress and billing-status views from BuildOps CRM exports each month, reconstructing budget variance in spreadsheets.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate BuildOps financial data to surface WIP, billing status, and budget variance, letting finance input projection assumptions in input tables and lock the period view.

writeback_model.name: Projection assumptions
writeback_model.description: where finance records forward-looking cost and billing inputs

writeback_model.name: Review status
writeback_model.description: the sign-off state of each project's WIP at close

use_cases.title: Change Order Tracking & Approval

use_cases.pain_point: Teams often manage change-order pricing, associations, and approval status across BuildOps and spreadsheets, with weak linkage between authorized changes and budget impact.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let teams log and associate change orders in input tables, route them for approval, and write authorized values back to update contract value and budget remaining.

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: who authorized each change order and the budget adjustment

use_cases.title: Labor Forecasting by Phase

use_cases.pain_point: PMs often forecast labor by phase and cost code in spreadsheets bolted onto project dashboards, making it hard to spot understaffed phases before they slip.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface labor demand from BuildOps data, let PMs enter forecasts by phase and cost code in input tables, and compare against actuals to flag variance early.

writeback_model.name: Labor forecasts
writeback_model.description: where PMs enter projected hours by phase and cost code

writeback_model.name: Variance flags
writeback_model.description: phases where forecast and actual labor diverge beyond threshold

use_cases.title: Purchase Order Closure Tracking

writeback_model.name: Closure status
writeback_model.description: the current open/closed state of each purchase order

use_cases.title: Estimating Workflow Workspace
use_cases.card_summary: Rebuild Excel-macro estimating in a governed workspace with reusable assumptions and writeback for forecast and bid data.
use_cases.pain_point: Firms often run forecast and estimating workflows through locked-down Excel macros and paginated BI tools reports, brittle and hard to maintain across estimators.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could rebuild estimating logic with input tables for quantities and rate assumptions, generate bid estimates, and write back versions for win-rate and accuracy analysis.

writeback_model.name: Bid versions
writeback_model.description: the saved estimate tied to each pursuit and its outcome

writeback_model.name: Status updates
writeback_model.description: the current state and owner of each submittal

use_cases.title: Field Operations Daily Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Consolidate job scheduling, project tracking, and vendor coordination from CRM field data into one operations workflow.

use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate CRM field data for jobs, vendors, and inventory, let teams update status in input tables, and surface daily operations KPIs.

use_cases.title: AI Territory & Bid Feedback Loop

use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface AI-scored opportunities, let reps classify and validate them in input tables with AI/Cortex assistance, and feed that labeled data back to refine the scoring model.

writeback_model.name: Score overrides

workflows.industry_context: PropTech platforms run rental performance benchmarking, vendor scorecards, leasing funnels, and compliance reporting on top of property-management-system and warehouse data. Many internal workflows still live in Retool forms, Google Sheets, and Smartsheets bolted onto the warehouse, where approvals, KPI targets, and operational actions need a governed, writeback- and API-capable home.

use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could use page controls and input tables as form inputs with buttons making API calls to cloud-function endpoints, letting support staff approve landlords, block accounts, or change limits directly from governed data.

workflows.industry_context: Property managers and REITs run monthly variance review, procurement and tenant-improvement tracking, tenant risk monitoring, and entity master-data management across large multi-market portfolios. These are multi-player, audit-sensitive processes where variance explanations, watch-list overrides, and entity creation still depend on spreadsheets and email bolted onto the warehouse and iLevel production data.

workflows.industry_context: Brokerages and listing platforms run on transactions, and every deal ends with a commission calculation involving tiered splits, team overrides, caps, referral fees, and franchise deductions. Much of this back-office work — plus marketing scenario planning and attribution correction — still runs on error-prone spreadsheets and disconnected tools, where calculation errors invite agent disputes and compliance exposure.
