use_cases.pain_point: BI project status is split between a ticketing tool and status decks, so stakeholders lack one view of progress against the fiscal calendar.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine project and issue data with the fiscal calendar, flag at-risk epics, and let owners annotate status into one portfolio view.

writeback_model.name: Status annotations
writeback_model.description: owner commentary on each project's progress and blockers

use_cases.title: Vendor Chargeback Dispute Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Track vendor compliance chargebacks, document disputes with evidence, and follow recovery to closure.
use_cases.pain_point: Vendor chargebacks and the disputes against them are managed in spreadsheets and email, so recoverable amounts and dispute status are hard to track.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface chargebacks by vendor, let teams document disputes and upload evidence, and follow each dispute to a reconciled outcome.

writeback_model.name: Dispute records
writeback_model.description: the reason and evidence for each contested chargeback

writeback_model.name: Recovery status
writeback_model.description: open, won, or lost state per dispute

use_cases.title: Product & Site Visibility Optimization
use_cases.card_summary: Combine product categorization with site visibility metrics and capture placement decisions to optimize merchandising.
use_cases.pain_point: Product placement decisions weigh categorization against site visibility metrics in spreadsheets, so the rationale behind placement changes is not retained.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine product categorization with site visibility metrics, surface placement gaps, and capture placement decisions and rationale to track impact.

writeback_model.name: Placement decisions
writeback_model.description: the proposed change and the visibility rationale behind it

workflows.industry_context: CPG companies run revenue growth management, trade spend, demand planning, and multi-channel P&Ls where pricing, forecast calls, and budgets are assembled at SKU and customer level in spreadsheets and BI side tools. Price discrepancies and trade fund leakage compound quietly across DTC, retail, wholesale, and marketplace channels.

use_cases.title: Price Discrepancy Exception Desk

value_stat.lbl: of revenue exposed to price errors

use_cases.card_summary: Monitor daily sales orders for pricing discrepancies, manage wholesale alerts, and resolve material pricing issues in a governed worklist.
use_cases.pain_point: CPG teams in this sector often catch price discrepancies after the order ships, patching wholesale alerts and material pricing errors in spreadsheets with no worklist.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could monitor daily orders for pricing discrepancies, surface high-value wholesale alerts and material pricing issues, and let teams manage resolution in input-table worklists.

writeback_model.name: Discrepancy worklist
writeback_model.description: each flagged pricing issue, owner, and resolution status

writeback_model.name: Resolution notes
writeback_model.description: the correction applied and rationale per discrepancy

use_cases.title: Trade Spend & Funds Control
use_cases.card_summary: Manage fund allocations across MDF and promotions, track actual vs budgeted spend, and forecast trade needs in one app.
use_cases.pain_point: Trade spend across MDF, special promotions, and discounts is managed in disconnected spreadsheets, so actual-versus-budget and fund balances are unclear until overspend appears.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let teams manage fund allocations across MDF, promotions, and discounts, input rates and assignments, and track actual versus budgeted trade spend with forecasting.

writeback_model.name: Fund assignments
writeback_model.description: the allocation, rate, and account for each trade fund

writeback_model.name: Spend adjustments
writeback_model.description: approved changes to fund allocation with rationale

use_cases.title: Field Forecast Call Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Let field reps write back forecast calls by business unit and brand against an evergreen plan-to-projection tracker.
use_cases.pain_point: Field forecast calls are collected by email and keyed into a finance spreadsheet, so the live picture of projected versus ordered net sales lags reality.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine product dimensions, historical demand, and projections, let field reps write back forecast calls in an input table, and track projected plus ordered net sales live.

writeback_model.name: Forecast calls
writeback_model.description: rep-entered net sales projections by business unit and brand

writeback_model.name: Projection status
writeback_model.description: percent ordered to call and days expired per period

use_cases.title: Annual Budget & Channel P&L Planning
use_cases.card_summary: Build SKU- and customer-level budgets with volume, price, COGS, and discounts, then compare actuals vs budget across channels.
use_cases.pain_point: Annual budgets are built at SKU and customer level in spreadsheets, then reconciled against actuals across DTC, retail, wholesale, and marketplace P&Ls by hand.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could integrate production sales with budget snapshots, let teams enter forecast data at SKU and customer level, and compare actuals versus budget across every channel.

writeback_model.name: Budget inputs
writeback_model.description: unit volume, price, COGS, and discount assumptions by SKU and customer

writeback_model.name: Budget snapshots
writeback_model.description: the locked budget version for actuals comparison

use_cases.title: Demand Planning Input Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Let planners input and adjust demand forecasts in one workflow that integrates with the existing ML forecast.
use_cases.pain_point: Demand planning spans Excel and BI tools, so planner overrides to the statistical forecast are made in spreadsheets disconnected from the model.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface the ML baseline forecast, let planners enter and reason about overrides in input tables, and publish a consensus demand plan that feeds downstream.

writeback_model.name: Forecast overrides
writeback_model.description: planner adjustments to the statistical forecast with rationale

writeback_model.name: Consensus plan
writeback_model.description: the agreed demand plan by SKU and period

use_cases.title: RGM Pricing Scenario Workspace
use_cases.card_summary: Combine historical and current pricing with forward forecasting to model revenue growth management scenarios.
use_cases.pain_point: RGM pricing analysis blends archived and current pricing with forward forecasts across spreadsheets, so price and pack scenarios cannot be compared or approved consistently.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine archived and current pricing with forward forecasting, let RGM teams model price and pack scenarios, and approve a pricing direction with full history.

writeback_model.name: Pricing scenarios
writeback_model.description: the modeled price and pack changes with projected impact

writeback_model.name: Approved pricing
writeback_model.description: the chosen pricing direction and approver

use_cases.title: Marketing Channel Reference Manager
use_cases.card_summary: Let users maintain marketing channel groupings in an input table instead of hard-coded case statements.
use_cases.pain_point: Marketing channel groupings are hard-coded in case statements, so every new campaign ID requires an engineering change instead of a business-user update.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let marketing maintain channel and campaign groupings in an input table that replaces hard-coded case logic and feeds downstream models dynamically.

writeback_model.name: Channel mappings
writeback_model.description: the campaign-ID-to-channel grouping maintained by business users

use_cases.title: SKU Master & Warehouse Setup
use_cases.card_summary: Manage SKU master data and warehouse stock-keeping setup with validation before records flow to operations.
use_cases.pain_point: SKU master setup for warehouse operations is handled in spreadsheets, so incomplete or inconsistent stock-keeping records reach operational systems.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let supply chain enter and validate SKU master attributes, flag incomplete records, capture approval, and sync clean data to warehouse operations via API action.

writeback_model.name: SKU setup records
writeback_model.description: new stock-keeping unit attributes entered for setup

writeback_model.name: Validation status
writeback_model.description: which records passed rules or need correction

use_cases.title: Customer Deduction & Claim Resolution
use_cases.card_summary: Work retailer deductions and trade claims, document validity with evidence, and track recovery to closure.
use_cases.pain_point: Retailer deductions and trade claims pile up in spreadsheets, so valid recoveries are missed and invalid deductions are written off without challenge.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface open deductions, match them to trade promotions, let teams document validity with evidence uploads, and track disputes and recovery to closure.

writeback_model.name: Deduction dispositions
writeback_model.description: the validity decision and evidence per deduction

writeback_model.name: Recovery status
writeback_model.description: open, recovered, or written-off state per claim

use_cases.title: Retail Sales & Syndicated Data Review
use_cases.card_summary: Combine retailer and syndicated sales data and capture analyst commentary on share and trend movements.
use_cases.pain_point: Retailer and syndicated data are analyzed in spreadsheets, so the insights behind share and trend swings live in side documents and get rebuilt each cycle.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine retailer and syndicated sales data, surface share and trend movements, and capture analyst commentary that informs category strategy.

writeback_model.name: Movement explanations
writeback_model.description: the documented driver behind each material share or trend swing

durable-consumer-goods-electronics-and-l.industry_name: Durable Consumer Goods, Electronics & Lifestyle Products

workflows.industry_context: Durable goods and electronics sellers manage dense SKU, listing, and marketplace data across Amazon and retail channels, where MSKU management, annual planning, and seasonal flow plans run through input-heavy workflows and spreadsheets. Lightweight CRM and reverse-ETL write-back patterns are common as teams orchestrate updates back to warehouse and external systems.

use_cases.title: Marketplace MSKU Management
use_cases.card_summary: Manage merchant SKU data, inventory, and costs across marketplace listings and write updates back to the warehouse.
use_cases.pain_point: Sellers in this sector often manage merchant SKU data, costs, and inventory across marketplace listings in spreadsheets, with no governed way to write updates back to the warehouse.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let users manage MSKU information against ASIN, inventory, and cost data, enter updates in input tables, and write changes back to the production warehouse.

writeback_model.name: MSKU updates
writeback_model.description: the merchant SKU attribute, cost, and inventory changes entered by users

writeback_model.name: Change log
writeback_model.description: a record of which MSKU records changed, by whom, and when

use_cases.title: Annual Sales Planning & Goal Setting
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze prior-year SKU performance and set next-year targets by subcategory and period in an interactive workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Annual planning analyzes sales, margin, and rankings across SKUs and subcategories, then targets are set in spreadsheets with no link back to the performance view.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface prior-year sales, contribution margin, and rankings, let teams set next-year targets by SKU and subcategory in an input table, and lock the approved goals.

writeback_model.name: Target inputs
writeback_model.description: next-year goals entered by SKU, subcategory, and period

writeback_model.name: Approved goals
writeback_model.description: the locked annual plan and approver

use_cases.title: Seasonal Flow Plan Approvals
use_cases.card_summary: Let merchandising and reps build, submit, and approve seasonal flow plans for product assortments in one workflow.
use_cases.pain_point: Seasonal flow plans for assortments are built and circulated in spreadsheets, so submissions, edits, and approvals lack a single governed workflow.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let planners and reps build seasonal flow plans, submit assortments, route review and approval, and publish the approved plan to supply chain.

writeback_model.name: Flow plan submissions
writeback_model.description: proposed seasonal assortments by rep and category

writeback_model.name: Approval decisions
writeback_model.description: the approved flow plan version and approver

use_cases.title: Lightweight CRM Write-Back
use_cases.card_summary: Create and update customer records in input tables that write back to the warehouse and sync to the CRM.
use_cases.pain_point: Teams maintain customer records across several tools, so updating an account means switching systems with no single place to create or edit records.
steps: Sync to CRM via reverse ETL
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let users create and update customer records in input tables that write back to the warehouse and sync to the CRM, building a lightweight CRM layer in Sigma.

writeback_model.name: Account updates
writeback_model.description: customer record changes entered by users

writeback_model.name: Sync status
writeback_model.description: which records have synced back to the CRM

use_cases.title: Opportunity Update Workflow
use_cases.card_summary: Update contract values and stage names against the pipeline and write changes back to the CRM via reverse ETL.
use_cases.pain_point: Updating opportunity values and stages means leaving the analytics view for the CRM, so reps toggle between systems to keep the pipeline current.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let reps edit contract values and stage names against the pipeline view in input tables and write changes back to the warehouse, syncing to the CRM via reverse ETL.

writeback_model.name: Opportunity edits
writeback_model.description: contract value and stage changes entered by reps

writeback_model.name: Sync log
writeback_model.description: the record of which edits synced to the CRM

use_cases.title: Marketplace Advertising ASIN Review
use_cases.card_summary: Analyze sponsored product and ASIN advertising performance and capture optimization notes per listing.
use_cases.pain_point: Marketplace advertising performance is analyzed per ASIN in spreadsheets, so bid and listing optimization decisions and their rationale are not retained.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface sponsored product and ASIN performance, let marketers annotate optimization actions, and track spend efficiency to inform the next bids.

writeback_model.name: Optimization notes
writeback_model.description: the documented action and rationale per ASIN

use_cases.title: Data Model Catalog & Governance
use_cases.card_summary: Maintain a searchable directory of data models with ownership and descriptions for governance and discoverability.
use_cases.pain_point: Data models across supply chain, sales, product, and store domains have no central directory, so ownership and relationships are unclear and rediscovered repeatedly.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could catalog data models with owner, description, and relationships in input tables, giving the org a governed, searchable directory.

writeback_model.name: Catalog entries
writeback_model.description: owner, description, and tags for each data model

use_cases.title: Employee Workload vs Sales Balancing
use_cases.card_summary: Compare employee workload to net sales by store, capture staffing adjustments, and track efficiency.
use_cases.pain_point: Employee workload and sales are analyzed in separate reports, so the relationship between staffing and store performance is reconstructed by hand.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine employee workload with net sales by store, flag imbalances, and let managers propose and approve staffing adjustments tracked to efficiency.

writeback_model.name: Staffing adjustments
writeback_model.description: proposed workload changes by store with rationale

writeback_model.name: Approval record
writeback_model.description: who approved each staffing change

use_cases.title: Product Return & RMA Tracker
use_cases.card_summary: Track product returns and RMAs, capture defect reasons with evidence, and feed quality insights back to product.
use_cases.pain_point: Returns and RMAs are logged in disconnected systems, so defect reasons and warranty cost are not connected to the products driving them.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let teams log returns and RMAs, capture defect reasons and evidence uploads, disposition units, and roll up quality insights by product.

writeback_model.name: Return records

writeback_model.name: Quality rollup
writeback_model.description: return and defect rates by product for feedback to product teams

use_cases.title: Channel Inventory Allocation Planner
use_cases.card_summary: Plan inventory allocation across marketplace, retail, and direct channels with override capture and approval.
use_cases.pain_point: Inventory allocation across marketplace, retail, and direct channels is planned in spreadsheets, so units get over-committed to one channel while another stocks out.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could surface available inventory, let planners propose channel allocations against demand priority, capture approval, and track sell-through by channel.

writeback_model.name: Allocation plan
writeback_model.description: units assigned to each channel and the priority reason

writeback_model.name: Approval record
writeback_model.description: who approved each channel allocation

workflows.industry_context: Food and beverage producers run replenishment, trade spend, and customer business review motions across syndicated (Nielsen/Circana) and retailer data, where collaborative planning, fund control, and QBR prep lean on spreadsheets and BI side tools. Bringing in clients' own syndicated data and managing trade funds are recurring ungoverned multi-player workflows.

use_cases.title: Trade Spend & Funds Control

use_cases.title: Retail & Syndicated Sales Analysis
use_cases.card_summary: Combine retailer and syndicated sales data and capture analyst commentary on market trends across channels.
use_cases.pain_point: Retailer and syndicated sales data are compared in spreadsheets, so cross-channel trend insights live in side documents and get rebuilt each cycle.

use_cases.title: Bring-Your-Own Syndicated Data Intake
use_cases.card_summary: Let clients upload and validate their own Nielsen and Circana syndicated data into a governed intake workflow.

use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could let clients upload their syndicated data, map it to a standard schema, validate it, and write clean records to the warehouse, replacing the janky form-based intake.

workflows.industry_context: Franchise retail and service chains coordinate workforce, events, and operations across many locations on a mix of POS (Square), payroll (Paylocity), and HR systems, where labor analytics, budget capture, and event pipelines run through dashboards bolted onto spreadsheets. Multi-location, multi-system reconciliation and franchise-specific data entry are recurring ungoverned workflows.

use_cases.title: Workforce Labor & Budget Variance
use_cases.card_summary: Combine payroll, time tracking, and revenue across locations to analyze labor efficiency and capture budget variance fixes.
use_cases.pain_point: Chains in this sector reconcile payroll, time tracking, and POS revenue across locations to assess labor efficiency, often in spreadsheets bolted onto disparate systems.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine payroll, time tracking, staffing, and revenue across locations, surface labor efficiency and budget variance, and let managers enter location-level fixes.

writeback_model.name: Variance fixes

writeback_model.name: Labor commentary
writeback_model.description: manager notes explaining efficiency outliers per location

use_cases.title: Franchise Events Pipeline & Budget

workflows.industry_context: Home goods makers and retailers run planning, forecasting, and budgeting across spreadsheets bolted onto warehouse data, often migrating off BI tools for governance. Demand, inventory, and marketing spend decisions cross sales, operations, and finance with weak audit trails and pseudo-writeback through Excel.

use_cases.title: Collaborative S&OP Demand Planning

workflows.industry_context: Semiconductor and hardware companies run capital-intensive planning, test, and supply-chain motions where small forecast or yield errors translate into millions in scrap and idle capacity. steps: Load MOF, BOH, and EGU data

use_cases.pain_point: Engineers often analyze wafer-level parameters and yield statistics in BI views, but the root-cause findings and committed corrective actions live in someone's head or a side email, so learnings aren't captured.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could sit beside wafer maps and parameter distributions, flag out-of-spec lots, let engineers capture root-cause annotations and corrective actions in input tables, and track them to closure.

use_cases.pain_point: Headcount allocation and roster data are often maintained in finance spreadsheets while hiring and role changes arrive over email, so plan-versus-actual drifts and there is no trail of allocation decisions.
use_cases.solution: A Sigma app could combine actual allocation data with forecasted roles and roster information, let HR and finance set allocation cycles and weights in input tables, and track headcount deltas across departments and cost centers.

writeback_model.description: the allocation weights and cycle assumptions applied per period

workflows.industry_context: Service marketplaces run dense provider-lifecycle and operational work across onboarding, activation, bookings, payouts, disputes, and policy enforcement that blends platform data with manual judgment. workflows.industry_context: Freight and shipping operators run dense billing, reconciliation, and tax-compliance work that blends data from multiple business units, service types, and warehouse systems with manual review and corrections.
